## [4.0.25] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî `adam_chat --trace` reflects the real runtime path (Codex v4.0.23 re-review #2)

Second patch on Codex's repeat external review. Closes finding #2 βÄî the pre-v4.0.25 `--trace` mode was materially false for every feature added after v4.0.20.

### Problem

`adam_chat --trace` manually re-implemented `Conversation::turn` so it could surface intermediate state, but stopped BEFORE calling `inject_retrieval_example` + `inject_reasoning_chain`. Consequence: trace always printed `reasoning_chain: None`, `example: None`, and fell through to `unknown.with_noun`, even when the real runtime produced a reasoning chain. Since auditability is a core contract for this project, this was a visible integrity gap.

### Fix

New public API on `Conversation`:

```rust
pub struct TurnTrace {
    pub parses: Vec<Analysis>,
    pub intent_after_injection: Intent,
    pub session_snapshot: HashMap<String, String>,
    pub plan_trace: Vec<String>,
}

pub fn turn_with_trace(&mut self, input, lex, repo, seed)
    -> (String, TurnTrace)
```

`turn_with_trace` is the new canonical implementation βÄî it runs the full pipeline (follow-up resolution βÜ£ retrieval injection βÜ£ reasoning-chain injection βÜ£ entity absorb βÜ£ plan βÜ£ realise) and returns the output **plus** the post-injection trace. The existing `turn` method is now a thin `let (out, _) = self.turn_with_trace(...); out` delegate βÄî no code duplication.

`adam_chat.rs --trace` now calls `turn_with_trace` directly and prints:
- FST parses
- `intent_after_injection` βÄî the real intent the planner saw (with `reasoning_chain` / `example` populated)
- session snapshot
- per-step `plan_trace`
- output

`TurnTrace` is re-exported from the dialog crate public surface so embedders can also consume it.

### Smoke-test

Pre-v4.0.25:
```
adam_chat --trace --once '“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄'
βÜ£ intent: Unknown { ..., noun_hint: Some("“¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄"), reasoning_chain: None, ... }
βÜ£ planner: template_key=unknown.with_noun
βÜ£ output: –Α―Ö, “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²–Α―¹―΄–Ζ –±–Α
```

But the non-trace run produced: `output: “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –Α–Ϋ―΄“¦―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ: –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ: “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ“™–Α “¦–Α―²―΄―¹―²―΄...`

Post-v4.0.25 (trace agrees with non-trace):
```
βî€βîÄ intent:   Unknown { ..., noun_hint: Some("“¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄"),
βî²                     example: Some("..."),
βî²                     reasoning_chain: Some("–Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ: “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ“™–Α “¦–Α―²―΄―¹―²―΄...") }
βî€βîÄ planner: template_key=unknown.with_derived_chain
βîîβîÄ output:   “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –Α–Ϋ―΄“¦―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ: –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ: “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ“™–Α “¦–Α―²―΄―¹―²―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―΄ –±–Α―Ä ...
```

Trace now matches real runtime output byte-for-byte.

### Cleanup

`adam_chat.rs` lost the now-unused `absorb_into` helper (~20 lines) and three stale imports (`interpret_text_with_lexicon`, `plan_response_with_session`, `realise`). The trace path is ~20 lines shorter and uses only the Conversation public API.

### Tests

**493 passing** (+1 regression `turn_with_trace_returns_post_injection_intent` βÄî asserts `reasoning_chain` is populated in the trace's intent).

### Scope

One concern βÄî trace auditability. No data / reasoner / extractor changes. `Conversation::turn` behaviour byte-identical (delegates to new `turn_with_trace`).

## [4.0.24] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Reranker tie-break fix (Codex v4.0.23 re-review #1)

First patch acting on Codex's v4.0.23 **repeat** external review. Closes finding #1 βÄî the v4.0.22 reranker still picked semantically weaker curated chains when multiple candidates tied at the top score.

### Problem

Codex re-review reproduced two cases where the v4.0.22 scorer produced a tied max-set:

- `adam_chat --once '–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄'` βÜ£ ¬Ϊ–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è“™–Α –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Φ“·―à–Β...¬Μ instead of the expected ¬Ϊ–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β βÄî –Α–¥–Α–Φ¬Μ. The tied set had IsA(–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β, –Α–¥–Α–Φ) + InDomain(–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β, –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è) + IsA(–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β, –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä) + IsA(–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β, ―¹“·―²“¦–Ψ―Ä–Β–Κ―²―•) etc. The canonical-triple tie-break (`.reverse()` picks lowest) surfaced the InDomain branch because ¬ΪInDomain¬Μ < ¬ΪIsA¬Μ lexicographically.

- `adam_chat --safe --once '–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄'` βÜ£ ¬Ϊ–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α βÄî –±–Α–Ι–Μ―΄“¦¬Μ (metaphor via proverb) instead of the expected ¬Ϊ–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α βÄî –±―•–Μ―•–Φ¬Μ (direct parent). The tied set had 4 fully-curated R1 IsA derivations (βÜ£ –±―•–Μ―•–Φ / –±–Α–Ι–Μ―΄“¦ / –Φ”ô–Μ―•–Φ–Β―² / “¦–Α–Ζ―΄–Ϋ–Α). Canonical triple picked –±–Α–Ι–Μ―΄“¦ because ¬Ϊ–±¬Μ < ¬Ϊ–±¬Μ ordered byte-wise first.

### Fix

Two new tie-break terms in `Conversation::inject_reasoning_chain`:

**1. IsA predicate bonus (+2) in `score_derivation`.** For "tell me about X" dialog queries an IsA answer (¬ΪX is a Y¬Μ) is the most semantically direct shape. Applied per-derivation so R1 IsA wins over R10 InDomain / R2 Has / R5 RelatedTo at the score level before canonical-triple fallback even runs.

**2. IsA-chain graph-distance BFS tie-break.** For two tied IsA derivations `(a IsA X)` vs `(a IsA Y)`, compute BFS depth from `a` to `X` and from `a` to `Y` walking **only base IsA facts** from `extracted_facts`. Shorter path wins. Base-only is critical βÄî including derived facts would make R1 transitive closure report every reachable object at depth 1, collapsing the distinction the tie-break needs.

```rust
fn isa_chain_depth(&self, subject: &str, target: &str) -> usize {
    // BFS over extracted_facts IsA edges, MAX_DEPTH=8, base-only.
    // Returns usize::MAX when unreachable so canonical-triple falls through.
}
```

### Smoke-test: both Codex cases resolved

| query | pre-v4.0.24 | post-v4.0.24 |
|---|---|---|
| `–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄` | –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è / –Φ“·―à–Β | **–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β βÄî –Α–¥–Α–Φ** β€™ |
| `–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄` (--safe) | ―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ / –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è | **–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β βÄî –Α–¥–Α–Φ** β€™ |
| `–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄` | –±–Α–Ι–Μ―΄“¦ (proverb metaphor) | **–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α βÄî –±―•–Μ―•–Φ** β€™ |
| `–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄` (--safe) | –±–Α–Ι–Μ―΄“¦ | **–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α βÄî –±―•–Μ―•–Φ** β€™ |

### Tests

**492 passing** (+2 regression tests):
- `reranker_prefers_is_a_over_other_predicates_on_tied_score` βÄî –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ > InDomain –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è.
- `reranker_prefers_shorter_is_a_path_on_tied_curated` βÄî synthetic 4-node IsA graph confirms depth-3 object is dropped in favour of depth-2 objects.

### Scope

One concern βÄî tie-break within Codex recommendation #3. No data / reasoner / extractor changes. Reranker scoring signature unchanged.

### Out of scope for this patch (Codex v4.0.23 re-review remainder)

- **#2 `--trace` mode** βÄî adam_chat.rs `--trace` path manually rebuilds the turn and stops before `inject_reasoning_chain` / `inject_retrieval_example`. Trace output is materially false for v4.0.20βÄ™v4.0.24 features. Queued for v4.0.25.
- **Residual: missing `world_core_multiword_coverage_test`** βÄî docstring at `semantics.rs:268` references this regression test but it doesn't exist. Queued for v4.0.26.

## [4.0.23] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî R5 overbroad-hub guard (Codex v4.0.19 review #4) βÄî final Codex-review patch

Fourth and final patch acting on external Codex review. Addresses finding #4: "―à–Η―Ä–Ψ–Κ–Η–Β ―Ö–Α–±―΄ –≤―Ä–Ψ–¥–Β `–Α–¥–Α–Φ`, `“™―΄–Μ―΄–Φ`, `–Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä` –¥–Α―é―² ―³–Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Μ―¨–Ϋ–Ψ –¥–Ψ–Ω―É―¹―²–Η–Φ―΄–Β, –Ϋ–Ψ –Ω―Ä–Α–≥–Φ–Α―²–Η―΅–Β―¹–Κ–Η ―¹–Μ–Α–±―΄–Β –≤―΄–≤–Ψ–¥―΄". R5 shared-IsA through an abstract "everything-is-one" hub produces pairs that are true but cognitively weak βÄî ¬Ϊ–Ψ―²―΄–Ϋ RelatedTo ―¹―É―¹―΄–Ϋ¬Μ because both IsA –Ζ–Α―², ¬Ϊ–Α―à―É RelatedTo –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―²¬Μ because both IsA ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ.

### Audit (pre-patch v4.0.22)

Data-driven classification of 15 621 R5 derivations by hub:

| hub | R5 pairs | verdict |
|---|---:|---|
| **–Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ** | 1 765 | information-bearing (profession cluster) βÄî keep |
| **“¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ** | 325 | information-bearing (tools) βÄî keep |
| ―¹–Α–Ϋ | 297 | numeric βÄî keep |
| **–Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä** | 183 | information-bearing (zoology) βÄî keep |
| ―²“·―¹ | 170 | information-bearing (colors) βÄî keep |
| ―²–Α“™–Α–Φ | 148 | information-bearing (food) βÄî keep |
| **―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ** | 135 | emotions βÄî keep (designed axis at v4.0.12) |
| **“¦“±–±―΄–Μ―΄―¹** | **135** | overbroad βÄî **block** |
| ―²―É―΄―¹ | 105 | information-bearing (kin) βÄî keep |
| –Κ”©–Μ―•–Κ | 88 | information-bearing (transport) βÄî keep |
| –Φ“·―à–Β | 77 | information-bearing (body parts) βÄî keep |
| ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―² | 77 | information-bearing (materials) βÄî keep |
| “¦“±―¹ | 66 | information-bearing (birds) βÄî keep |
| **”ô―Ä–Β–Κ–Β―²** | **66** | overbroad βÄî **block** |
| ―΄–¥―΄―¹ | 54 | information-bearing (vessels) βÄî keep |
| **–±–Β–Μ–≥―•** | **45** | overbroad βÄî **block** |
| **–Ζ–Α―²** | **20** | overbroad (most abstract "thing") βÄî **block** |
| βÄΠ | βÄΠ | βÄΠ |
| **–Α–¥–Α–Φ** | ~adjusted ~400 via cross-cluster bridges | overbroad per Codex βÄî **block** |

### Fix

New `is_overbroad_r5_hub(root)` guard in `reasoner.rs`. Blocks 5 semantically-abstract hubs: **–Ζ–Α―², –±–Β–Μ–≥―•, ”ô―Ä–Β–Κ–Β―², “¦“±–±―΄–Μ―΄―¹, –Α–¥–Α–Φ**. Applied at the R5 hub-iteration site βÄî skips the whole hub before enumerating incoming-IsA pairs.

```rust
fn is_overbroad_r5_hub(root: &str) -> bool {
    matches!(root, "–Ζ–Α―²" | "–±–Β–Μ–≥―•" | "”ô―Ä–Β–Κ–Β―²" | "“¦“±–±―΄–Μ―΄―¹" | "–Α–¥–Α–Φ")
}
```

The –Α–¥–Α–Φ inclusion is the biggest design call. Codex named it explicitly. The kin cluster (v4.0.19 batch) linked through ¬Ϊ―²―É―΄―¹ IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ¬Μ bridge, and R5 generated ~400 cross-cluster pairs like ¬Ϊ–Α–Ϋ–Α RelatedTo –Ε–Ψ–Μ–Α―É―à―΄¬Μ (mother related to passenger βÄî weak). Blocking –Α–¥–Α–Φ at R5 preserves touch-chain IsA knowledge but stops the combinatorial fan-out.

–Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ / –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä / “™―΄–Μ―΄–Φ (which Codex also named) are **kept** βÄî those hubs do produce meaningful pairs. ¬Ϊ–Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ζ RelatedTo –Ϋ–Α―É–±–Α–Ι―à―΄¬Μ (cook βÜî baker) is cognitively useful; ¬Ϊ–Α―¹―²―Ä–Ψ–Ϋ–Ψ–Φ–Η―è RelatedTo –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α¬Μ is domain-adjacent. The distinction is information-bearing (–Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ = specific profession type) vs. overbroad (–Α–¥–Α–Φ = "any human").

### Measured delta

| | v4.0.22 | v4.0.23 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| **R5 shared_is_a_target** | **15 621** | **13 566** | **βà£2 055 (βà£13.2 %)** |
| **derivations total** | 19 395 | **17 340** | **βà£2 055** |
| R1βÄ™R4, R6βÄ™R11 | unchanged | unchanged | 0 |
| Graph nodes / edges | 3 515 / 13 725 | 3 515 / 13 725 | 0 (base facts unchanged) |

### Tests

**490 passing** (+2 regression tests: `r5_skips_overbroad_hubs` verifies all 5 blocked hubs, `r5_still_fires_for_information_bearing_hubs` verifies 5 preserved hubs βÄî –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ, –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä, “¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ, “™―΄–Μ―΄–Φ, ―²“·―¹).

### Scope

One concern βÄî R5 source-level noise filter on 5 overbroad hubs. No extractor / data / rendering / reranker changes. Base fact graph unaffected.

### Codex v4.0.19 review βÄî 4/4 completed

| recommendation | patch | status |
|---|---|---|
| #1 Lexicon sync | v4.0.20 | β€Ö done |
| #2 Multi-word entity linker | v4.0.21 | β€Ö done |
| #3 Reasoning chain reranker | v4.0.22 | β€Ö done |
| #4 Tighten broad-hub rule guards | v4.0.23 | β€Ö done |
| #5 Learned component | βÄî | out of scope per `project_v4_direction` |
| #6 Generator model | βÄî | out of scope per `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2` |

| facts.json total | 13 745 | **15 448** | +1 703 |

Derivations **2.2Ο½** baseline after Codex-review cleanup removed overbroad R5 noise.

## [4.0.22] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Reasoning chain reranker (Codex v4.0.19 review #3)

Third patch acting on external Codex review. Replaces the "first match wins" derivation picker in `inject_reasoning_chain` with a scored ranker that prefers **curated + short + taxonomically-direct** chains and penalises **text-only + long + shared-target fan-out** derivations.

### Problem

Per Codex's v4.0.19 review, `Conversation::inject_reasoning_chain` selected the first derivation whose subject (then object) root matched the `noun_hint`. This is deterministic but semantically arbitrary βÄî when multiple derivations exist for the same noun, the picker surfaced noisy ones:

- ¬Ϊ–Α–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ –Κ“·―à–Κ–Β “¦–Α―²―΄―¹―²―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―΄ –±–Α―Ä¬Μ βÄî weak chain when cleaner curated alternatives available
- ¬Ϊ–Α–±–Α–Ι βÄî ―Ö–Α–Μ―΄“¦¬Μ (pre-v4.0.2) βÄî text-only IsA chain when world_core ¬Ϊ–Α–±–Α–Ι βÄî –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ¬Μ existed
- ¬Ϊ“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• βÄî –Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α¬Μ βÄî weak proverb chain when R1 ¬Ϊ“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• βÄî –±–Β–Μ–≥―•¬Μ (curated from language_features.jsonl) existed

### Fix βÄî `score_derivation` composite scoring

New `fn score_derivation(d, noun) -> i32` at `conversation.rs:525`. Composite score terms:

**Trust (source_chain provenance):**
- All sources `world_core/*`: **+4** (fully curated)
- Mixed world_core + text: +1
- All text-only: **βà£2**
- Empty chain (defensive): βà£2

**Chain length:**
- 0βÄ™1 sources: +2
- 2 sources: +1
- 3+: 0 (long chains drift)

**Rule weight (Codex ordering):**
- `R1_is_a_transitivity`, `R10_in_domain_inheritance`: **+3** (clean taxonomic)
- `R2`, `R3`, `R6`, `R7`, `R8`, `R9`: +2 (mereological/temporal)
- `R5_shared_is_a_target`, `R11_in_domain_shared_target`: **+1** (combinatorial fan-out βÄî last resort)

**Subject-side preference** (preserves pre-v4.0.22 subject-first picking): +1 if subject root matches `noun`.

Tie-break by canonical triple `(subject, predicate, object)` for deterministic byte-identical runs.

### Selection flow

```
filter:   noun-match on either subject or object + passes_safety (curated_only_reasoning gate)
reduce:   max_by(score_derivation, then canonical-triple reverse tie-break)
render:   render_derivation_as_kazakh (unchanged)
```

Result: for the same noun, a fully-curated R1 chain (score βâà 10) always beats a mixed-source R5 chain (score βâà 4).

### Smoke-test with `adam_chat --safe`

All curated-only picks are now surfacing their strongest chain:

```
> –Α–±–Α–Ι ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: “¦–Ψ―Ä―΄―²―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄: –Α–±–Α–Ι βÄî –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ (–±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄).
> –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
–Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –Α–Ϋ―΄“¦―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ: –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² –Ω–Β–Ϋ –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―à –±―•―Ä-–±―•―Ä―•–Ϋ–Β –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ.
> –Α–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: –Α–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ –Β―É―Ä–Α–Ζ–Η―è“™–Α “¦“±―Ä–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –±―•―Ä –±”©–Μ―•–≥―• ―Ä–Β―²―•–Ϋ–¥–Β ―à―΄“¦―²―΄.
> “ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
–ê–Ι―²―É―΄“Θ―΄–Ζ –±–Ψ–Ι―΄–Ϋ―à–Α, –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι “¦–Η―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –±–Α―Ä: “¦–Ψ―Ä―΄―²―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄: “¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• βÄî –±–Β–Μ–≥―• (–±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄).
```

Before v4.0.22 the first query often produced ¬Ϊ–Α–±–Α–Ι βÄî ―Ö–Α–Μ―΄“¦¬Μ (text noise); the last query produced ¬Ϊ“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• βÄî –Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α¬Μ (proverb metaphor). Now both pick curated R1 chains.

### Tests

**488 passing** (+2 regression tests: `reranker_prefers_curated_over_text_only`, `reranker_prefers_shorter_chain`).

### Scope

One concern βÄî derivation-selection ranking. No reasoner/extractor/data changes. Rendering layer (`render_derivation_as_kazakh`) untouched.

## [4.0.21] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Multi-word entity linker (Codex v4.0.19 review #2)

Second release acting on Codex's v4.0.19 review. Addresses finding #2: multi-word concepts in world_core (¬Ϊ“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄¬Μ, ¬Ϊ–ö“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•¬Μ, ¬Ϊ–ê―¹–Ω–Α–Ϋ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β―¹―•¬Μ, ¬Ϊ“ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―•¬Μ, βÄΠ) were losing their referent at the dialog layer because the FST tokenizer splits the compound and `first_noun_root` picks only the first single-word token βÄî so ¬Ϊ“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄¬Μ replied about ¬Ϊ“¦“±―¹¬Μ (bird) instead of –€–Μ–Β―΅–Ϋ―΄–Ι –Ω―É―²―¨.

### Fix

Added `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` const array in `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` βÄî **22 compound entities** auto-extracted from `data/world_core/*.jsonl` subjects/objects that contain a space. Sorted longest-first at compile time so the matcher returns on the first substring hit:

```
“¦“±–Ι―Ä―΄“¦―²―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ, “¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ”ô–¥–Β–±–Η–Β―²―•, ―²―•―Ä―à―•–Μ―•–Κ –Η–Β―¹―•, –Ψ―Ä―²–Α–Μ―΄“¦ –Α–Ζ–Η―è,
–Ε“·–Κ –Φ–Α―à–Η–Ϋ–Α―¹―΄, –Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ϋ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β―¹―•, “¦–Α―Ä–Α ―¹”©–Ζ–¥–Β―Ä, ―²–Α“™―΄ –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä, “¦–Ψ–Ζ―΄ –Κ”©―Ä–Ω–Β―à,
“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―•, ―¹―É “¦–Ψ–Ι–Φ–Α―¹―΄, –Ε–Β―Ä –±–Β–¥–Β―Ä―•, –Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•, ―²―É“™–Α–Ϋ –Ε–Β―Ä,
–Α–±–Α–Ι –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄, ―²–Β–Φ―•―Ä –Ε–Ψ–Μ, “¦―΄–Ζ –Ε―•–±–Β–Κ, –±–Α―¹ –Κ–Η―•–Φ, “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄, –Α―è“¦ –Κ–Η―•–Φ,
―¹–Α―Ä―΄ –Φ–Α–Ι, ―²–Α―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ
```

New helper `multiword_entity_hint(input: &str) -> Option<String>` does a longest-match substring scan over the lowercased raw input. Substring match handles Kazakh inflection on the compound tail βÄî e.g. ¬Ϊ“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄–Ϋ―΄“Θ –±–Β–Ι–Ϋ–Β―¹―•¬Μ contains ¬Ϊ“¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄¬Μ as a prefix of the inflected form.

New `best_noun_hint(input, parses)` combines: **multi-word match first, then `first_noun_root` fallback**. Called from `interpret_text_with_lexicon` at both `Intent::Unknown` exit points. The legacy `interpret(parses)` function (no raw input available) keeps single-noun-only behaviour βÄî documented in-line.

### Smoke-test: compound entities now answer correctly

Pre-v4.0.21 βÄî all of these answered about "“¦“±―¹" (bird) or generic:
```
> “ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄ βÜ£ ... about “¦“±―¹ (bird)
```

Post-v4.0.21:
```
> “ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ: “¦“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ“™–Α “¦–Α―²―΄―¹―²―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―΄ –±–Α―Ä (–Η–Β–Μ―•–Κ –Φ“±―Ä–Α–≥–Β―Ä–Μ―•–Κ).
> –ö“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―• –Ε–Α–Ι–Μ―΄
–Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―• ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –Α–Ϋ―΄“¦―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ: –Α–Ι –Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•–≥–Β “¦“±―Ä–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –±―•―Ä –±”©–Μ―•–≥―• ―Ä–Β―²―•–Ϋ–¥–Β ―à―΄“¦―²―΄.
> –ê―¹–Ω–Α–Ϋ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β―¹―• –¥–Β–≥–Β–Ϋ –Ϋ–Β
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: –Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ϋ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β―¹―• –Ω–Β–Ϋ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ –±―•―Ä-–±―•―Ä―•–Ϋ–Β –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ.
> “ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―•
–ê–Ι―²―É―΄“Θ―΄–Ζ –±–Ψ–Ι―΄–Ϋ―à–Α, –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι “¦–Η―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –±–Α―Ä: “¦–Ψ―Ä―΄―²―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄: “¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―• βÄî –Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α (–±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄).
```

¬Ϊ“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄¬Μ now correctly connects to stars (Milky Way contains stars). ¬Ϊ–ö“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•¬Μ produces the curated moon-as-part-of-solar-system chain. Only ¬Ϊ“ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―²―•–Μ―•¬Μ still picks a weak proverb-chain βÄî that's **Codex recommendation #3** (reasoning reranker), explicitly out of scope for this patch.

### Tests

**486 passing** (+2 regression tests: `multiword_entity_hint_matches_compound_entities` + `multiword_entity_hint_returns_none_for_simple_input`).

### Scope

One concern βÄî multi-word entity resolution at the dialog layer. No reasoner / extractor changes, no data changes. The multi-word list is hand-maintained; future world_core compound additions must extend the const array (add a `world_core_multiword_coverage_test` regression in a future patch is queued).

## [4.0.20] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Lexicon sync with World Core (Codex v4.0.19 review #1)

First release acting on Codex's external review of v4.0.19. Codex's diagnosis was: **knowledge exists in the graph but doesn't reach the user through the dialog layer**. Root cause #1 βÄî many `world_core` subject/object roots are not in the Lexicon, so `first_noun_root` (dialog's entry point) returns None and the query falls through to ¬Ϊ―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ¬Μ.

### Audit findings

Cross-checking `data/world_core/*.jsonl` single-word subjects/objects against the Lexicon (curated `segmentation_roots.json` + Apertium import):

- **295 world_core roots missing from the Lexicon** βÄî including core vocabulary (–Α–Ι, ―¹―É, “¦–Α–Ϋ, “¦―΄–Ζ, “¦–Ψ–Μ, –±–Α―¹, –±–Β―² βÄî surprisingly absent) and every recent v4.0.9+ domain-authored root (–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β, –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―², –¥–Ψ–Φ–±―΄―Ä–Α, –Φ–Β–¥–±–Η–Κ–Β, –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α, –Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ω, –±–Α“™―΄―², ”©–Μ―à–Β–Φ, etc.).

### Fix βÄî one concern, with a caveat

Added **270 roots** to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` with auto-classified vowel-harmony + final-sound-class via a heuristic script (Kazakh last-vowel harmony rule + final-char sound class). Roots all flagged with `v4020` id prefix for grep-ability of provenance.

**Filter βÄî 25 roots deferred**: first attempt added all 295, which broke 4 tokenizer-contract tests (seg_253 –Α–Μ–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ, seg_282 “¦―΄―¹“¦–Α, seg_320 –±–Α―¹“¦–Α βÄî short-root collisions with existing affix parses). Reverted and filtered to **length βâΞ 4 chars + NOT in a homograph risk-list** (–Α–Ι, ―¹―É, –Α“¦, –Β–Ϋ, ―²―É, –Α–Μ, “¦–Α–Ϋ, “¦–Ψ–Μ, –±–Α―¹, –±–Β―², –Φ–Α–Μ, –Κ–Β―à, “¦―΄―¹, –Ψ“Θ, ―¹–Ψ–Μ, –Ω–Β―à, ―¹”©–Ζ, ―²–Α–Μ, ―²“·―¹, –Φ―΄―¹, “¦–Α–Ζ, “¦–Α―Ä, “¦―΄–Ζ, –±–Α―É, –Α–Μ–Α). These 25 need per-root FST priority handling in a future patch βÄî one-concern discipline defers.

### Smoke-test: dialog now answers previously-silent queries

Pre-v4.0.20:
```
> –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ
> –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ
> –¥–Ψ–Φ–±―΄―Ä–Α ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ
> –Φ–Β–¥–±–Η–Κ–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
―²“·―¹―•–Ϋ–±–Β–¥―•–Φ
```

Post-v4.0.20 (all 4 now produce curated-derived answers):
```
> –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: “¦–Ψ―Ä―΄―²―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄: –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β βÄî –Α–¥–Α–Φ (–±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄).
> –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
–Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –Α–Ϋ―΄“¦―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ: –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―² –Ω–Β–Ϋ –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―à –±―•―Ä-–±―•―Ä―•–Ϋ–Β –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ.
> –¥–Ψ–Φ–±―΄―Ä–Α ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
“ö–Ψ–Μ–¥–Α –±–Α―Ä –¥–Β―Ä–Β–Κ―²–Β―Ä–¥–Β–Ϋ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ “¦“±―Ä–Α―¹―²―΄―Ä–¥―΄–Φ: “¦–Ψ―Ä―΄―²―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄: –¥–Ψ–Φ–±―΄―Ä–Α βÄî “¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ (–±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Ψ–Ι-―²―•–Ζ–±–Β–Κ –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄).
> –Φ–Β–¥–±–Η–Κ–Β ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Α–Ι―²―à―΄
–ê–Ι―²―É―΄“Θ―΄–Ζ –±–Ψ–Ι―΄–Ϋ―à–Α, –Φ―΄–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ι “¦–Η―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄ –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹ –±–Α―Ä: –Φ–Β–¥–±–Η–Κ–Β –Ω–Β–Ϋ –Φ–Β―Ä–≥–Β–Ϋ –±―•―Ä-–±―•―Ä―•–Ϋ–Β –±–Α–Ι–Μ–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ.
```

This is the **highest-impact single patch** of v4.0.x so far βÄî it converts existing knowledge into actually-reachable answers.

### Measured delta on T4_200k full re-extract

|---|---:|---:|---|
| Lexicon curated roots | 4 432 | **4 702** | **+270** |

| text `related_to` | 1 458 | **1 957** | **+499** |
| text `goes_to` | 1 537 | **1 681** | +144 |

| **R8 after_transitivity** | 734 | **999** | **+265** |
| **R5 shared_is_a_target** | 15 477 | **15 621** | +144 |

| R6 lives_in_via_part_of | 49 | 81 | +32 |

| Graph edges | 12 360 | **13 725** | **+1 365 (+11 %)** |

### Why such large extract jump (+1 739 text facts)

Kazakh sentences involving the 270 new roots were previously **parseable only partially** βÄî e.g. a sentence mentioning ¬Ϊ–Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β –Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•¬Μ would fail at the noun analysis, so downstream pattern matchers never fired. With the roots in Lexicon, every such sentence is now extractable. The +955 `does_to` gain is the largest βÄî agent_verb patterns are the most common sentence shape in the Wikipedia + textbook corpus, and they were blocked wherever the subject or object noun was one of the newly-added roots.

### Tests

**484 passing** (unchanged βÄî Lexicon addition didn't break any existing test after the filter was tightened).

| Tests | 463 | **484** | +21 |

**Derivations 2.5Ο½ baseline.**

### Not in scope (queued)

- v4.0.21: Codex recommendation #2 βÄî longest-match entity linker for multiword concepts (¬Ϊ“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄¬Μ βÜ£ galaxy, not just ¬Ϊ“¦“±―¹¬Μ).
- v4.0.22: Codex recommendation #3 βÄî reasoning chain reranker (curated-first, short-first, R1/R10-first).
- v4.0.23: Codex recommendation #4 βÄî tighten rule guards on broad hubs (–Α–¥–Α–Φ / “™―΄–Μ―΄–Φ / –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä).
- Deferred: 25 short / homograph-prone roots (–Α–Ι, ―¹―É, –Α“¦, etc.) βÄî need per-root FST priority handling.

## [4.0.19] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî World Core batch #5: `kinship_extended.jsonl` + `constellations_kz.jsonl` + `measurements.jsonl` (R5 explodes via –Α–¥–Α–Φ bridge)

Fifth data batch. **Highest single-batch leverage ever**: +67.6 derivations per curated fact (previous peak: v4.0.9's +47/fact via 40-entry professions.jsonl saturating –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ hub).

### Three new domains

1. **`kinship_extended.jsonl`** (18 entries) βÄî extended Kazakh family terms. Hub: `―²―É―΄―¹ IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ` (kin IsA human βÄî the load-bearing bridge). 17 ―²―É―΄―¹ children: –Α―²–Α / ”ô–Ε–Β part_of –Ψ―²–±–Α―¹―΄ + IsA ―²―É―΄―¹, –Α“™–Α / ―•–Ϋ―• / –Α–Ω–Α / “¦–Α―Ä―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α―¹ / ―¹―•“Θ–Μ―• / –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β―Ä–Β / ―à”©–±–Β―Ä–Β / –Ε–Η–Β–Ϋ / –±”©–Μ–Β / –Ϋ–Α“™–Α―à―΄ / –Α–±―΄―¹―΄–Ϋ / –Κ“·–Ι–Β―É / –Κ–Β–Μ―•–Ϋ IsA ―²―É―΄―¹, plus “±–Μ / “¦―΄–Ζ IsA –±–Α–Μ–Α. Standard Kazakh kinship lexicon, no loanwords.

2. **`constellations_kz.jsonl`** (6 entries) βÄî traditional Kazakh astronomy. `―à–Ψ“¦–Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ IsA –Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ϋ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β―¹―•` hub + 4 constellation children: –•–Β―²―•“¦–Α―Ä–Α“¦―à―΄ (Ursa Major βÄî "seven thieves"), “°―Ä–Κ–Β―Ä (Pleiades), –Δ–Β–Φ―•―Ä“¦–Α–Ζ―΄“¦ (Polaris βÄî "iron stake", IsA –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ), “¦“±–Ι―Ä―΄“¦―²―΄ –Ε“±–Μ–¥―΄–Ζ (comet βÄî "tailed star"). Plus `“ö“±―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ―΄ IsA –≥–Α–Μ–Α–Κ―²–Η–Κ–Α` (Milky Way βÄî confirms implicit usage in astro_022).

3. **`measurements.jsonl`** (10 entries) βÄî physical measurement concepts. `”©–Μ―à–Β–Φ IsA –±–Β–Μ–≥―•` hub + 9 measurement children IsA ”©–Μ―à–Β–Φ: “±–Ζ―΄–Ϋ–¥―΄“¦, –Κ”©–Μ–Β–Φ, ―¹–Α–Μ–Φ–Α“¦, –±–Η―•–Κ―²―•–Κ, ―²–Β―Ä–Β“Θ–¥―•–Κ, –Β–Ϋ, “¦–Α―à―΄“¦―²―΄“¦, –Ε―΄–Μ–¥–Α–Φ–¥―΄“¦, –Ω–Α–Ι―΄–Ζ.

### Totals

| | v4.0.18 | v4.0.19 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| World Core domains | 26 | **29** | +3 |
| World Core entries | 792 | **826** | +34 |
| World Core facts | 886 | **922** | +36 (kin_002 / kin_003 produce 2 facts each: part_of –Ψ―²–±–Α―¹―΄ + IsA ―²―É―΄―¹) |

### Measured runtime delta (fast-path rebuild)

| rule | v4.0.18 | v4.0.19 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1 is_a_transitivity | 484 | **568** | **+84** |
| **R2 has_inheritance** | 450 | **707** | **+257** |
| R3 has_via_part_of | 51 | 51 | 0 |
| **R5 shared_is_a_target** | 13 414 | **15 477** | **+2 063** |
| R6 lives_in_via_part_of | 49 | 49 | 0 |
| R7 goes_to_via_part_of | 373 | 373 | 0 |
| R8 after_transitivity | 734 | 734 | 0 |
| R9 part_of_transitivity | 170 | **172** | +2 |
| **R10 in_domain_inheritance** | 102 | **124** | **+22** |
| R11 in_domain_shared_target | 146 | **151** | +5 |
| **derivations total** | 15 973 | **18 406** | **+2 433 (+15.2 %)** |
| Graph nodes | 3 452 | **3 472** | +20 |
| Graph edges | 12 325 | **12 360** | +35 |

### Effective leverage: +67.6 derivations per curated fact βÄî new peak

**2 433 new derivations / 36 new curated facts = +67.6/fact** βÄî exceeds v4.0.9's +47/fact peak. The combinatorial explosion is driven by one specific fact: `–Κ–Η–Ϋ_001: ―²―É―΄―¹ IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ`. This single bridge connects the entire 17-child kin cluster into the large –Α–¥–Α–Φ IsA hub. Every kin child βÜ£ IsA ―²―É―΄―¹ βÜ£ R1-transitively IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ βÜ£ R5-related to every other IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ descendant (including all professions via –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ–Η―è, all animals, etc.).

### R5 explosion breakdown (rough)

- 17 kin children Ο½ each becomes IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ via R1 transitive closure
- –Α–¥–Α–Φ hub pre-batch already had ~60+ descendants (indirect via IsA chains through –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ, etc.)
- 17 Ο½ 60 new R5 pairs βâà ~1 000 from cross-cluster pairs
- Plus C(17,2) = 136 intra-kin pairs
- Plus second-order cascades
- **Observed +2 063** βÄî consistent with bridge-fact multiplier effect

### R2 jump (+257) explanation

With kin cluster now IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ via R1, and `–Α–¥–Α–Φ has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ` (from emotions.jsonl at v4.0.12), R2 derives ¬ΪX has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ¬Μ for every kin child βÄî 17+ new Has-inheritance derivations. Plus `–Α–¥–Α–Φ has –Κ”©–Ζ / “¦“±–Μ–Α“¦ / –Φ–Η / –Ε“·―Ä–Β–Κ / “¦–Α–Ϋ / ”©–Κ–Ω–Β / –±–Α―É―΄―Ä / –±“·–Ι―Ä–Β–Κ / –Α―¹“¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ / ―¹–Α―É―¹–Α“¦ / –Α―è“¦ / “¦–Ψ–Μ` (from body_parts.jsonl) βÄî each kin child inherits all these via R2. 17 Ο½ ~12 body parts = ~200 R2 derivations. Rest from R1-chained ―Ü–Β–Ω–Ψ―΅–Κ–Η.

### Lesson: bridge facts multiply

This batch demonstrates the **highest-ROI authoring pattern**: a single `X IsA –±–Ψ–Μ―¨―à–Ψ–Ι_―Ö–Α–±` bridge fact can multiply existing cluster connectivity by C(cluster_size, 2). v4.0.9's professions.jsonl did this via –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ hub; v4.0.19 does it via –Α–¥–Α–Φ hub + kin cluster. Future high-leverage authoring: look for uncovered sub-hubs that could link into –Α–¥–Α–Φ / –Ζ–Α―² / –Φ“·―à–Β / “¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ with minimal curation.

## [4.0.18] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî R11 InDomain shared-target (new reasoning rule) + v4.0.17 fragment-fix materialised

Third rule-axis patch in v4.0.x. Reasoner roster **9 βÜ£ 10**. Also materialises the v4.0.17 is_closed_class fragment expansion via full T4_200k re-extract.

### Pattern

`A InDomain D βàß B InDomain D (A βâ† B) βüΙ RelatedTo(A, B)` βÄî identical structural shape to R5 (shared-IsA), applied to the InDomain predicate.

### Why InDomain-shared

After v4.0.14's R10 inheritance rule, the graph has rich InDomain coverage: 24 base + 102 R10-derived = **126 InDomain facts**. Each domain hub has multiple incoming InDomain edges:

- –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α: ~26 incoming βÜ£ C(26,2) = 325 candidate pairs
- –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è: ~22 incoming βÜ£ C(22,2) = 231
- ”ô–¥–Β–±–Η–Β―²: ~18 incoming βÜ£ C(18,2) = 153
- –Ψ―Ä–Ϋ–Η―²–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è: ~13 incoming βÜ£ C(13,2) = 78

Many of these candidate pairs are already dedup'd against R5-derived shared-IsA pairs (since domain children often share taxonomic parents), so R11's net contribution is the **cross-cluster pairs** that aren't reachable via IsA alone.

### Implementation

`rule_r11_in_domain_shared_target` in `reasoner.rs` βÄî ~40-line body, structurally identical to R5 but scans incoming InDomain edges. Guards:

- **Tautology**: A = B rejected (canonical pair after sort).
- Standard `source_chain` + `rule_id: "R11_in_domain_shared_target"` + `ConfidenceKind::RuleInferred`.

### Test coverage

5 new regression tests:

- `r11_derives_related_to_from_shared_domain` βÄî basic 2-child hub (“¦–Ψ―¹―É/–±”©–Μ―É InDomain –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α).
- `r11_respects_tautology_guard` βÄî duplicate InDomain facts produce no self-related.
- `r11_does_not_fire_for_distinct_domains` βÄî A InDomain X + B InDomain Y produces nothing.
- `r11_produces_canonical_pair_once` βÄî C(3,2) = 3 unique canonical pairs.
- `r11_chains_through_r10_derived_in_domain` βÄî confirms R11 fires on R10-derived InDomain at fixpoint iter 2.

### Measured delta on T4_200k full re-extract + reasoner

| | v4.0.16 | v4.0.18 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 715 | **13 673** | **βà£42** (v4.0.17 fragment-fix materialised) |
| text `does_to` | 9 002 | **8 987** | βà£15 |
| text `goes_to` | 1 544 | **1 537** | βà£7 |
| text `lives_in` | 288 | **280** | βà£8 |
| text `has` | 230 | **224** | βà£6 |
| text `after` | 219 | **218** | βà£1 |
| **derivations total** | 15 832 | **15 973** | **+141 (+0.89 %)** |
| R2 has_inheritance | 454 | **450** | βà£4 (dedup cascade from fewer base has) |
| R7 goes_to_via_part_of | 374 | **373** | βà£1 |
| **R11 in_domain_shared_target** | βÄî | **146** | **new** |
| R1 / R3 / R5 / R6 / R8 / R9 / R10 | unchanged | unchanged | 0 |
| Graph nodes | 3 456 | **3 452** | βà£4 |
| Graph edges | 12 368 | **12 325** | βà£43 |

### v4.0.17 fragment-fix materialised

Full re-extract applied v4.0.17's `is_closed_class` fragment expansion (–Ε–Α–Μ–Ω, –Φ“±–Ϋ, –Α―¹―²–Α, ―Ö―•―Ö) βÄî net **βà£42 text-extracted facts** across 5 predicates, confirming v4.0.17's predicted "~32 facts cleaned" was accurate (slight under-prediction due to cascade through other matchers sharing the is_closed_class filter).

### R11 measured 146 net derivations

Pre-rule audit on v4.0.14 predicted R10+R11 stack would produce hundreds of shared-InDomain pairs. Observed net 146 βÄî well below the theoretical maximum because **most candidate pairs dedup against R5-derived shared-IsA pairs**. R5 already covers arithmetic/biology/literature sibling relations through shared taxonomic parents (e.g. `“¦–Α―Ä“™–Α IsA “¦“±―¹ + –Α“¦“¦―É IsA “¦“±―¹` βüΙ R5 produces `“¦–Α―Ä“™–Α RelatedTo –Α“¦“¦―É` before R11 can). R11's unique contribution is the **cross-cluster pairs** βÄî concepts sharing a domain but NOT a direct IsA parent (e.g. `–Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α` InDomain-children that aren't IsA-siblings: ―¹–Α–Ϋ vs “¦–Ψ―¹―É vs –Β―¹–Β–Ω βÄî each under different IsA parents but same domain).

### Tests

**484 passing** (+5 R11 regression from v4.0.17).

## [4.0.17] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Fragment roots in `is_closed_class` (code-only micro-patch)

Follow-up to v4.0.16's noise audit. While cleaning location-root GoesTo subjects, the audit also surfaced 4 fragment / tokenisation-artefact roots contaminating text-extracted facts:

| root | Ο½ | origin |
|---|---:|---|
| `–Ε–Α–Μ–Ω` | 12 | fragment of ¬Ϊ–Ε–Α–Μ–Ω―΄¬Μ (generally) βÄî FST over-segments before ―²–Ψ“¦―΄-reduction rule |
| `–Φ“±–Ϋ` | 8 | demonstrative stem fragment (¬Ϊ–Φ“±–Ϋ―΄¬Μ / ¬Ϊ–Φ“±–Ϋ–¥–Α¬Μ stripped to stem) |
| `–Α―¹―²–Α` | 7 | fragment of ¬Ϊ–Α―¹―²–Α–Φ¬Μ (more than) |
| `―Ö―•―Ö` | 5 | tokenised Roman numeral XIX |

v4.0.6 already blocked 3 fragment roots (`–Ε–Α―Ä―²`, `–Α―Ä–Α―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α“™`, `―²–Α“™―΄`); v4.0.17 extends the same blocklist pattern to these 4. Total combined: ~32 base facts will be filtered on the next full re-extract.

### Code change

4-line extension to the `is_closed_class` match + 1 new regression test. Regression test also asserts non-collision with legitimate neighbours: ¬Ϊ–Ε–Α–Μ–Ω―΄¬Μ (full form), ¬Ϊ–Α―¹―²–Α–Ϋ–Α¬Μ (city-root βÄî must not collide with fragment ¬Ϊ–Α―¹―²–Α¬Μ), ¬Ϊ–Φ“±–Ϋ–¥–Α¬Μ (full locative).

### Delivery discipline: code-only, no re-extract

v4.0.16 consumed a 26-minute full T4_200k re-extract to materialise its location-root fix. This patch is small enough (~32 expected base-fact reductions) that a dedicated re-extract is wasteful. **Committed `facts.json` retains the ~32 fragment facts until the next full re-extract** βÄî planned for v4.0.18 along with a new reasoning rule that'll also benefit from the cleaner base.

### Tests

**479 passing** (+1 regression `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_17_fragments` from v4.0.16).

### Scope

One concern: expand `is_closed_class` with 4 fragments. No data changes, no other code changes.

## [4.0.16] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Noise audit #2: location-root subjects in `dative_goes_to` + `agent_verb`

Second noise-elimination audit of v4.0.x. Audit on fresh v4.0.15 derived_facts.json surfaced a major contamination class: **R7 GoesTo-via-PartOf had 385 of 388 derivations either fully text-only or mixed** βÄî traced back to text-extracted GoesTo base facts with country / city subjects.

### Audit findings

R7 provenance breakdown on v4.0.15 (388 derivations):

| provenance | count | share |
|---|---:|---:|
| fully world_core | 3 | 0.8 % |
| mixed | 338 | 87.1 % |
| fully text-only | 47 | 12.1 % |

**R7 is the most text-dependent rule in the reasoner** βÄî it needs both a GoesTo base and a PartOf base, and GoesTo is predominantly text-extracted.

Top text-extracted GoesTo subjects (all producing R7 cascade noise):

| root | Ο½ | kind |
|---|---:|---|
| “¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ | 52 | ethnic noun / proper noun (homograph) |
| –Α–¥–Α–Φ | 27 | generic subject (metaphorical usage) |
| **“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ** | **22** | **country βÄî location, not agent** |
| **–Α–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄** | **20** | **city βÄî location, not agent** |
| **―à―΄“™―΄―¹** | **12** | direction (now curated in directions.jsonl) |
| –Ε–Α–Μ–Ω | 12 | fragment of –Ε–Α–Μ–Ω―΄ |
| **―¹–Ψ–Μ―²“·―¹―²―•–Κ** | **8** | direction (now curated) |
| **–Α“¦―²”©–±–Β / –Ω–Α–≤–Μ–Ψ–¥–Α―Ä / –Α―Ä–Α–Μ** | each **7** | **cities** |

Bolded rows total **~80 base facts** that are clearly locations appearing as kinetic-verb subjects βÄî from Wikipedia biographical patterns like ¬Ϊ–û“Θ―²“·―¹―²―•–Κ “ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ –Ψ–±–Μ―΄―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–≥–Β –Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•¬Μ ("was born in South Kazakhstan oblast") that the extractor takes as `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ goes_to –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β`.

### Root cause (consistent with v4.0.10's pattern)

Four matchers produce predicates whose subjects should not be location nouns (`LivesIn`, `GoesTo`, `DoesTo`):

- `locative_lives_in` β€™ (has `is_location_root` guard since v3.8.5)
- `dative_goes_to` β€½ **missing the guard**
- `agent_verb` (DoesTo) β€½ **missing the guard**
- `copula_is_a` βÄî N/A (IsA can legitimately have location subjects like `–Ε–Β―Ä IsA “™–Α–Μ–Α–Φ―à–Α―Ä`)

v3.8.5 hardening identified location nouns as a noise class for `locative_lives_in` but didn't extend to the kinetic verb matchers βÄî the same oversight pattern that v4.0.10 fixed for `is_time_noun` on `copula_is_a`.

### Fix βÄî one concern

Added `is_location_root(&root.root)` guard after the existing `is_time_noun` / < 3-char filter in both:

1. **`dative_goes_to`** subject (line ~567 in patterns.rs)
2. **`agent_verb`** subject (line ~995 in patterns.rs)

Plus 2 new regression tests:

- `dative_goes_to_rejects_location_subject` βÄî 3 Wikipedia-style cases (“ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ, –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄, –ê“¦―²”©–±–Β).
- `agent_verb_rejects_location_subject` βÄî 2 Wikipedia-style cases (“ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ, –†–Β―¹–Β–Ι).

### Measured delta (full re-extract T4_200k + reasoner)

| | v4.0.15 | v4.0.16 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 925 | **13 715** | **βà£210** |
| text-extracted `does_to` | ~9 171 | **9 002** | **βà£169** (agent_verb location-subject guard) |
| text-extracted `goes_to` | ~1 590 | **1 544** | **βà£46** (dative_goes_to location-subject guard) |
| **derivations total** | 15 846 | **15 832** | βà£14 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 388 | **374** | **βà£14** (primary R-rule target) |
| R1-R6, R8-R10 | unchanged | unchanged | 0 |
| Graph nodes | 3 461 | **3 456** | βà£5 |
| Graph edges | 12 495 | **12 368** | **βà£127** |
| R7 provenance split | 3 WC / 338 mixed / 47 text | 3 WC / 326 mixed / 45 text | mixed βà£12, text βà£2 |

**Noise-leverage discrepancy vs v4.0.10**: v4.0.10's `copula_is_a` time-noun guard produced **5.7 derivations eliminated per base fact** (63 base βÜ£ 357 deriv). v4.0.16 produces only **0.065 deriv/base** (215 base βÜ£ 14 deriv). Reason: location-subject `goes_to` / `does_to` base facts rarely fed R7 chains because their destinations (–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β, “¦–Α“™–Α–Ζ, ”©―¹–Β–Κ, –Κ”©“Θ―•–Μ, etc. βÄî Wikipedia biographical metonymy) lacked matching `part_of` targets in the graph. The primary win here is **direct base-fact precision** βÄî 215 categorically wrong text extractions ("“ö–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–≥–Β –Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•" βÜ£ `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦―¹―²–Α–Ϋ goes_to –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β`) removed βÄî not rule cascade reduction.

### Tests

**478 passing** (+2 regression tests from v4.0.15).

### Not in scope (queued)

- **¬Ϊ“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦¬Μ Ο½ 52** text GoesTo βÄî ethnic-noun / homograph polysemy (Qazaq city in Azerbaijan). Same class as v4.0.10's ¬Ϊ–Α–±–Α–Ι IsA –Α―É―΄–Μ¬Μ deferral βÄî needs dialog-layer sense disambiguation, not extractor guard.
- **Fragment roots** ¬Ϊ–Ε–Α–Μ–Ω / –Φ“±–Ϋ / –Α―¹―²–Α / ―Ö―•―Ö¬Μ Ο½ 35 combined βÄî v4.0.6 closed-class expansion pattern; one-concern discipline defers to a future patch.
- **¬Ϊ–Α–¥–Α–Φ¬Μ Ο½ 27**, **¬Ϊ–±–Α–Μ–Α¬Μ Ο½ 15** βÄî generic human subjects; often legitimate ("person goes to work"). Semantic filtering needed, not a blanket guard.

### Cumulative v4.0.7 βÜ£ v4.0.16 (10 releases)

| | v4.0.7 | v4.0.16 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Active reasoning rules | 7 | 9 | +2 (R9, R10) |
| World Core domains | 14 | **26** | +12 |
| World Core entries | 549 | **792** | +243 |
| facts.json total | 13 745 | **13 715** | **βà£30** (cleaner after v4.0.10 / v4.0.16 noise fixes) |
| Derivations | 7 866 | **15 832** | **+7 966 (+101.3 %)** |
| Tests | 463 | **478** | +15 |

v4.0.x has now accumulated **two noise-elimination milestones** (v4.0.10 time-nouns in `copula_is_a`, v4.0.16 location-nouns in `dative_goes_to` + `agent_verb`) βÄî both closing 2-year-old oversights where v3.8.5 hardening extended a guard to some matchers but missed others.

## [4.0.15] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî World Core batch #4: `language_features.jsonl` + `cooking_methods.jsonl` + `directions.jsonl`

Fourth data batch. Three more curated domains, chosen to exploit R9 (PartOf-transitivity, v4.0.13) and R10 (InDomain-inheritance, v4.0.14) by feeding them long part_of chains and populous IsA taxonomies.

### Three new domains

1. **`language_features.jsonl`** (18 entries) βÄî linguistic structure. 5-hop part_of backbone: `–¥―΄–±―΄―¹ βÜ£ –±―É―΄–Ϋ βÜ£ ―¹”©–Ζ βÜ£ ―¹”©–Ι–Μ–Β–Φ βÜ£ –Φ”ô―²―•–Ϋ βÜ£ ―²―•–Μ`. Sub-chains: `”ô―Ä―•–Ω part_of –Ε–Α–Ζ―É part_of ―²―•–Μ`, `–Φ–Α“™―΄–Ϋ–Α part_of ―¹”©–Ζ`. Sound types: `–¥–Α―É―΄―¹―²―΄ / –¥–Α―É―΄―¹―¹―΄–Ζ IsA –¥―΄–±―΄―¹` (vowels/consonants). Action verbs: `―¹”©–Ι–Μ–Β―É / –Ε–Α–Ζ―É IsA ”ô―Ä–Β–Κ–Β―²`. 4 –±–Β–Μ–≥―• children: `–±―É―΄–Ϋ / ”ô―Ä―•–Ω / ―¹”©–Ι–Μ–Β–Φ` IsA –±–Β–Μ–≥―•.

R9 jumps from 117 βÜ£ **170 (+53)** βÄî 10 new part_of entries produce **+5.3 R9 derivations per entry**. Plus cross-activation: R3/R6/R7 each gained ~8 derivations from R9's new part_of facts.

## [4.0.14] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî R10 InDomain-inheritance via IsA (new reasoning rule)

Second consecutive rule-axis patch. Reasoner roster 8 βÜ£ 9. Pattern: `A IsA B βàß B InDomain D βüΙ A InDomain D` βÄî identical shape to R2 (Has-inheritance), applied to the domain-membership predicate.

### Why InDomain-inheritance

InDomain has been the least-activated predicate βÄî only 24 base facts on v4.0.13 (14 in kz_literature, 4 math-ops, plus biology/anatomy/astronomy/color seeds). Yet IsA taxonomies are dense (587 distinct subjects). An inheritance rule unlocks coverage through existing taxonomy without new curation: every –±ird inheriting –Ψ―Ä–Ϋ–Η―²–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è from `“¦“±―¹ InDomain –Ψ―Ä–Ϋ–Η―²–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è`, every number inheriting –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α from `―¹–Α–Ϋ InDomain –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α`.

### Pre-rule audit on v4.0.13

Direct 1-hop chains available (A IsA B βàß B InDomain D, no trivial skip):

| domain | derivable count |
|---|---:|
| –Φ–Α―²–Β–Φ–Α―²–Η–Κ–Α | 25 |
| –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è | 21 |
| –Ψ―Ä–Ϋ–Η―²–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è | 12 |
| ”ô–¥–Β–±–Η–Β―² | 4 |
| –Α―¹―²―Ä–Ψ–Ϋ–Ψ–Φ–Η―è | 3 |
| –Κ”©―Ä―É | 1 |
| **total 1-hop** | **66** |

Plus fixpoint chaining through R1-derived IsA facts (e.g. `–Α―Ä―΄―¹―²–Α–Ϋ IsA –Ε―΄―Ä―²“¦―΄―à IsA –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä` βÜ£ R1 derives `–Α―Ä―΄―¹―²–Α–Ϋ IsA –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä` βÜ£ R10 derives `–Α―Ä―΄―¹―²–Α–Ϋ InDomain –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è` at iter 2).

### Measured on committed v4.0.13 runtime

| rule | v4.0.13 | v4.0.14 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1-R9 rules | unchanged | unchanged | 0 |
| **R10_in_domain_inheritance** | βÄî | **102** | **new** |
| **derivations total** | 14 836 / 15 033 | **15 135** | **+102 (+0.68 %)** |
| Fixpoint passes | 5 | 5 | same |

**102 > 66 predicted** βÄî the 36-fact delta is R1-transitive chaining at iter 2. When `X IsA Y IsA Z` exists and `Z InDomain D`, R10 fires for both `(X, InDomain, D)` and `(Y, InDomain, D)` after R1 produces the `X IsA Z` shortcut. Classic fixpoint compounding.

### R10 is isolated (no cross-activation)

Unlike R9 which fed into R3/R6/R7 via PartOf, R10 produces InDomain facts that no current rule consumes. Future R11/R12 could extend (e.g. ¬ΪA InDomain D1 βàß B InDomain D1 βüΙ RelatedTo(A, B)¬Μ βÄî the InDomain analogue of R5 shared-IsA), but that's scope for a later patch.

### Implementation

`rule_r10_in_domain_inheritance` in `reasoner.rs` βÄî same ~30-line structure as R2 Has-inheritance. Guards:

- **Tautology**: `A = D` rejected (defensive; would mean A categorized into itself via a taxonomy hop).
- **No cross-scale guard**: InDomain is not a scale concept.
- Standard `source_chain` + `rule_id: "R10_in_domain_inheritance"` + `ConfidenceKind::RuleInferred`.

### Test coverage

5 new regression tests:

- `r10_derives_in_domain_inheritance` βÄî basic 1-hop (“¦–Α―¹“¦―΄―Ä IsA –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä βÜ£ InDomain –Ζ–Ψ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–≥–Η―è).
- `r10_respects_tautology_guard` βÄî synthetic A IsA B + B InDomain A rejection.
- `r10_does_not_fire_without_chain` βÄî isolated InDomain fact alone βÜ£ no derivation.
- `r10_dedupes_against_existing_fact` βÄî explicit long-arc β΅£ R10 doesn't duplicate.
- `r10_chains_through_r1_derived_is_a` βÄî 3-level chain –Α―Ä―΄―¹―²–Α–Ϋ IsA –Ε―΄―Ä―²“¦―΄―à IsA –Ε–Α–Ϋ―É–Α―Ä, confirms R10 fires on R1-derived IsA at fixpoint iter 2.

### Tests

**476 passing** (+5 R10 regression tests from v4.0.13).

## [4.0.13] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî R9 PartOf-transitivity (new reasoning rule)

Rule-axis rotation after three consecutive data batches. The reasoner has been at 7 active rules since v4.0.4 (R8 added); v4.0.13 adds the 8th βÄî **R9 PartOf-transitivity**.

### Why PartOf-transitivity specifically

`PartOf` is a partial order. The transitive closure is **mathematically clean** βÄî no semantic overreach, unlike `Has`-transitivity which was rejected in v2.x because "car has wheel βàß garage has car βüΙ garage has wheel" is false. Mereological part-of chains do compose: ¬Ϊ―à–Α―à part_of –±–Α―¹ βàß –±–Α―¹ part_of –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β βüΙ ―à–Α―à part_of –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β¬Μ is universally accepted.

### Why the timing makes sense

Three v4.0.x data batches (v4.0.7, v4.0.9, v4.0.11, v4.0.12) populated the `PartOf` base from 117 to 137 facts across plants / house_parts / body_parts / transport / astronomy. Pre-rule audit surfaced **103 ready 2-hop chains** on the committed graph βÄî enough for R9 to produce meaningful output on day one, unlike the v2.4.0 R1-activation (which fired 0 times until v2.5+ data landed).

### Implementation

New rule in `adam-reasoning/src/reasoner.rs` (~30-line body, same structure as R8). Guards:

- **Tautology**: `A = C` rejected (defensive; well-formed PartOf chains are acyclic).
- **Astronomical cross-scale**: inherited from the R6/R7 pattern βÄî if target `C` is an astronomical-scale object (`is_astronomical_object`) and subject `A` is not, reject. Prevents future ¬Ϊ–Ε–Α–Ω―΄―Ä–Α“¦ part_of –Α“™–Α―à part_of ... part_of –Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•¬Μ leaks once intermediate forest / ecosystem entries land.
- Standard `source_chain` + `rule_id: "R9_part_of_transitivity"` + `ConfidenceKind::RuleInferred`.

### Measured delta on committed v4.0.12 runtime

| rule | v4.0.12 | v4.0.13 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 473 | 473 | 0 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 467 | 454 | **βà£13** (dedup βÄî see below) |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 28 | **43** | **+15 (+54 %)** |
| R5_shared_is_a_target | 12 791 | 12 791 | 0 |
| R6_lives_in_via_part_of | 37 | **41** | +4 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 306 | **380** | **+74 (+24 %)** |
| R8_after_transitivity | 734 | 734 | 0 |
| **R9_part_of_transitivity** | βÄî | **117** | **new** |
| **derivations total** | 14 836 | **15 033** | **+197 (+1.3 %)** |
| Fixpoint passes | 6 | **5** | cleaner convergence |

### Cross-activation, not just direct derivation

The 117 direct R9 derivations are only ~60 % of the net gain. R9 creates new PartOf facts that **R3**, **R6**, **R7** can then chain through βÄî R7 alone gained +74 derivations (+24 %) as motion-through-parts chains deepened one hop. R3 Has-via-PartOf gained +15 (+54 % on a rule that was previously sparsely activated). This is a **rule-on-rule multiplier** βÄî the intended effect for a mereological primitive.

The R2 drop (βà£13) is dedup: R9's new part_of derivations mean R2 convergence picks up facts at a different iteration, so some Has-inheritance derivations get consolidated earlier. Fixpoint in 5 passes (was 6) confirms cleaner convergence.

### Test coverage

Six new regression tests in `reasoner.rs`:

- `r9_derives_part_of_transitivity` βÄî basic 2-hop (―à–Α―à βÜ£ –±–Α―¹ βÜ£ –¥–Β–Ϋ–Β).
- `r9_respects_tautology_guard` βÄî synthetic cyclic chain rejection.
- `r9_astronomy_same_scale_allowed` βÄî –Ε–Β―Ä βÜ£ –Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―• βÜ£ –≥–Α–Μ–Α–Κ―²–Η–Κ–Α passes.
- `r9_astronomy_cross_scale_rejected` βÄî synthetic ¬Ϊ–±–Α–Μ–Α part_of –Ε–Β―Ä part_of –Κ“·–Ϋ –Ε“·–Ι–Β―¹―•¬Μ blocked.
- `r9_chains_across_iterations` βÄî 4-node chain (―²―•―¹/–Α―É―΄–Ζ/–±–Β―²/–±–Α―¹/–¥–Β–Ϋ–Β) reaches full transitive closure (6 non-adjacent pairs).
- `r9_dedupes_against_existing_fact` βÄî explicit long-arc in input β΅£ R9 doesn't re-derive.

### Tests

**471 passing** (+6 R9 regression tests from v4.0.12).

### Noise propagation (honest baseline)

R9 propagates existing noise in the PartOf base βÄî e.g. ¬Ϊ―²–Β“Θ―•–Ζ part_of ”©―¹―•–Φ–¥―•–Κ part_of –Κ”©–Κ”©–Ϋ―•―¹¬Μ (text-extraction chain, semantically absurd) will produce ¬Ϊ―²–Β“Θ―•–Ζ part_of –Κ”©–Κ”©–Ϋ―•―¹¬Μ as a derivation. This is **the same invariant all rules carry**: the reasoner doesn't validate base-fact semantics. The `derivation_is_fully_curated` helper (v4.0.3) remains the recommended filter for investor-safe surfaces.

## [4.0.12] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî World Core batch #3: `emotions.jsonl` + `weather_phenomena.jsonl` + `materials.jsonl`

Substituted with `materials.jsonl` βÄî genuine gap (―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―² hub had zero world_core coverage).

1. **`emotions.jsonl`** (18 entries) βÄî abstract-concept domain. Opens with `–Α–¥–Α–Φ has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ` (activates R2 Has-inheritance through `X IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ` chains). 17 emotion types IsA ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ: “¦―É–Α–Ϋ―΄―à, “¦–Α–Ι“™―΄, –Α―à―É, –Φ–Α―Ö–Α–±–±–Α―², “¦–Ψ―Ä“¦―΄–Ϋ―΄―à, ―²–Α“Θ–¥–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹, “·–Φ―•―², ”©–Κ―•–Ϋ―•―à, –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―à, “±―è―², ―΄–Ζ–Α, ―¹–Α“™―΄–Ϋ―΄―à, –Φ–Β–Ι―•―Ä―•–Φ, ―¹–Β–Ϋ―•–Φ, ―Ä–Η–Ζ–Α―à―΄–Μ―΄“¦, ―Ä–Β–Ϋ―•―à, –±–Α“¦―΄―². Pure native Kazakh, no loanwords (―ç–Φ–Ψ―Ü–Η―è / –Ω―¹–Η―Ö–Η–Κ–Α / ―¹―²―Ä–Β―¹―¹ all skipped).

Adds 15 atmospheric + seismic phenomena: –Ε–Α“Θ–±―΄―Ä, “¦–Α―Ä, –±“±―Ä―à–Α“¦, ―²“±–Φ–Α–Ϋ, ―à―΄“¦, –Ε–Β–Μ, –±–Ψ―Ä–Α–Ϋ, –¥–Α―É―΄–Μ, –Ϋ–Α–Ι–Ζ–Α“™–Α–Ι, ―¹–Β–Μ, –Ζ―•–Μ–Ζ–Α–Μ–Α, “¦―É–Α“Θ, “¦―΄―Ä–Α―É, –Α―è–Ζ, –±“±–Μ―². –ö–Β–Φ–Ω―•―Ä“¦–Ψ―¹–Α“¦ deliberately NOT duplicated (already in colors.jsonl).

Metals (―²–Β–Φ―•―Ä, –Φ―΄―¹, –Α–Μ―²―΄–Ϋ, –Κ“·–Φ―•―¹, “¦–Ψ―Ä“™–Α―¹―΄–Ϋ, ―à–Ψ–Ι―΄–Ϋ, –±–Ψ–Μ–Α―²), minerals (―²–Α―¹, ―¹–Α–Ζ), organic materials (“¦–Α–Ι―΄―¹, ―²–Β―Ä―•, –Φ–Α―²–Α, –Ε―•–Ω). Cross-chain designed-in: `–Φ–Α―²–Α IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²` in this batch + existing `–Ε“·–Ϋ / –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α / –Ε―•–±–Β–Κ IsA –Φ–Α―²–Α` from `clothing.jsonl` βÜ£ R1 transitivity produces ¬Ϊ–Ε“·–Ϋ IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²¬Μ etc. without explicit statement.

### Effective leverage: +19 derivations per added curated fact

Below v4.0.11's +27/fact and v4.0.9's peak of +47/fact. Explanation: this batch adds **three small isolated hubs** (―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ with 17 children, ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―² with 13, + 15 new “¦“±–±―΄–Μ―΄―¹ children) rather than **one large cross-chain** into the existing –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ hub. R5 shared-IsA leverage scales as C(n,2) within a hub βÄî 17-child ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ gives C(17,2) = 136 pairs; 13-child ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―² gives 78; 15 –Ϋ–Ψ–≤―΄―Ö “¦“±–±―΄–Μ―΄―¹ children + 2 pre-existing (―²―•―Ä―à―•–Μ―•–Κ, –Κ–Β–Φ–Ω―•―Ä“¦–Ψ―¹–Α“¦) = 17 total, giving C(17,2) = 136 pairs of which ~15Ο½2 = 30 are new from this batch. Total new R5: roughly 136 + 78 + 30 + cross-hub trickles + R1/R2 cascades βâà 851 βÄî matches observed.

### R2 activation via ¬Ϊ–Α–¥–Α–Φ has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ¬Μ

New fact `–Α–¥–Α–Φ has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ` triggers R2 Has-inheritance for every curated `X IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ` chain. Current state has few direct `IsA –Α–¥–Α–Φ` entries; leverage will compound as future batches add human-category children.

### Cross-domain cross-chain designed-in

- `–Φ–Α―²–Α IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²` (materials) + existing `–Ε“·–Ϋ / –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α / –Ε―•–±–Β–Κ IsA –Φ–Α―²–Α` (clothing) βÜ£ R1 transitive `–Ε“·–Ϋ IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²`, `–Φ–Α“¦―²–Α IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²`, `–Ε―•–±–Β–Κ IsA ―à–Η–Κ―•–Ζ–Α―²` emerge without explicit statement.
- `–Α–¥–Α–Φ has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ` (emotions) + future `–Α–¥–Α–Φ IsA X` entries will produce R2 `X has ―¹–Β–Ζ―•–Φ` inheritance.

### Pipeline cost

Full rebuild: ~3 s. Pre-v4.0.8 equivalent: ~135 min = **~2 700Ο½ speedup** on 3-domain batch.

### Cumulative v4.0.7 βÜ£ v4.0.12 (6 releases)

| | v4.0.7 | v4.0.12 | cumulative delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| World Core domains | 14 | **23** | +9 (+64 %) |
| World Core entries | 549 | **755** | +206 (+37.5 %) |
| World Core facts | 643 | **849** | +206 (+32.0 %) |
| Derivations | 7 866 | **14 836** | **+6 970 (+88.6 %)** |
| R5 shared-IsA | 5 940 | **12 791** | **+6 851 (+115 %)** |
| Graph nodes / edges | 3 315 / 12 350 | 3 432 / 12 495 | +117 / +145 |
| Pipeline cost per data patch | ~45 min | **~3 s** | ~900Ο½ faster |

### Scope

Purely additive data. No code changes. 465 tests unchanged.

A dedicated education.jsonl would duplicate ~10 of 15 core entries. Substituted with `house_parts.jsonl` βÄî genuine gap (“·–Ι / –±”©–Μ–Φ–Β / –Ε–Η“Μ–Α–Ζ had zero world_core coverage pre-v4.0.11).

## [4.0.10] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî Noise-elimination audit: time-noun subjects in `copula_is_a`

Audit on the fresh v4.0.9 `derived_facts.json` (12 849 derivations) surfaced one dominant text-only noise class that had persisted through v4.0.x: Wikipedia timeline entries extracted as IsA facts with month / day / year subjects.

### Audit findings

R5 provenance breakdown on v4.0.9 (10 827 shared-IsA derivations):

| provenance | count | share |
|---|---:|---:|
| both sources world_core | 9 293 | 85.8 % |
| mixed (1 world_core + 1 text) | 1 421 | 13.1 % |
| both sources text | 113 | 1.0 % |

**R5 is already safe.** 85.8 % fully curated; the mixed path is filtered by `derivation_is_fully_curated` in the dialog layer (v4.0.2 / v4.0.3).

**R1 is_a-transitivity** told a different story βÄî 19 of 449 derivations were **fully text-only**. Inspecting those, every single one traced back to one of three noise classes:

1. **Month-name subjects from Wikipedia timelines** βÄî "8 “¦–Α“Θ―²–Α―Ä βÄî –ê“¦―²”©–±–Β–¥–Β –ö–Β“Θ–Β―¹ ”©–Κ―•–Φ–Β―²―• –Ψ―Ä–Ϋ–Α–¥―΄" βÜ£ `“¦–Α“Θ―²–Α―Ä IsA ”©–Κ―•–Φ–Β―²―•`. Classes: `“¦–Α“Θ―²–Α―ÄΟ½4`, `–Α“¦–Ω–Α–ΫΟ½1`, `―¹”ô―É―•―ÄΟ½2`, `“¦―΄―Ä–Κ“·–Ι–Β–ΚΟ½1`, `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α–ΫΟ½3`, `–Ε–Β–Μ―²–Ψ“¦―¹–Α–ΫΟ½2` βÄî 13 base IsA facts.
2. **Year subject `–Ε―΄–Μ`** βÄî "1791 –Ε―΄–Μ βÄî –½―΄―Ä―è–Ϋ –Κ–Β–Ϋ―•―à―²–Β―Ä―• –Ε“±–Φ―΄―¹―΄–Ϋ―΄“Θ –±–Α―¹―²–Α–Μ―É―΄" βÜ£ `–Ε―΄–Μ IsA –Ε“±–Φ―΄―¹―΄–Ϋ`. 15+ base facts from date-prefixed timeline entries.
3. **Month-to-month ranges in parens** βÄî "(“¦―΄―Ä–Κ“·–Ι–Β–Κ 1955 βÄî ―¹”ô―É―•―Ä 1963) –ë–Ψ–±–Η―Ä –ù." βÜ£ `“¦―΄―Ä–Κ“·–Ι–Β–Κ IsA ―¹”ô―É―•―Ä`, `―¹”ô―É―•―Ä IsA “¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ`, etc.

### Root cause

Of the four v2.x-era IsA-producing matchers (`copula_is_a`, `locative_lives_in`, `dative_goes_to`, `agent_verb`), three already applied an `is_time_noun` subject guard. **`copula_is_a` did not.** It was the only matcher whose subject path went through `resolve_bare_noun` without any time-noun filter. Every other matcher had the guard added in v3.8.5 when time nouns were identified as a noise class for `LivesIn`/`GoesTo`/`DoesTo`; the `copula_is_a` oversight was missed.

### Fix βÄî one-concern patch

1. **Expanded `is_time_noun`** with 19 new entries βÄî 12 months (“¦–Α“Θ―²–Α―Ä, –Α“¦–Ω–Α–Ϋ, –Ϋ–Α―É―Ä―΄–Ζ, ―¹”ô―É―•―Ä, –Φ–Α–Φ―΄―Ä, –Φ–Α―É―¹―΄–Φ, ―à―•–Μ–¥–Β, ―²–Α–Φ―΄–Ζ, “¦―΄―Ä–Κ“·–Ι–Β–Κ, “¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ, “¦–Α―Ä–Α―à–Α, –Ε–Β–Μ―²–Ψ“¦―¹–Α–Ϋ) + 7 days (–¥“·–Ι―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•, ―¹–Β–Ι―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•, ―¹”ô―Ä―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•, –±–Β–Ι―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•, –Ε“±–Φ–Α, ―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•, –Ε–Β–Κ―¹–Β–Ϋ–±―•). Seasons deliberately excluded: –Κ”©–Κ―²–Β–Φ / –Ε–Α–Ζ / –Κ“·–Ζ / “¦―΄―¹ are curated in world_core.time.jsonl as legitimate IsA subjects (e.g. `–Ε–Α–Ζ IsA –Φ–Β–Ζ–≥―•–Μ`) and never appeared as text-extraction noise.
2. **Added `is_time_noun(&subj.root)` guard** to `copula_is_a` after `resolve_bare_noun`.
3. **Two new regression tests**: `is_time_noun_covers_v4_0_10_months_and_days` (31 assertions) and `copula_is_a_refuses_time_noun_subject` (5 Wikipedia-style negative cases).

### Homograph handling

Three of the month names are homographs with other Kazakh words: `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ` (October / cauldron), `–Φ–Α–Φ―΄―Ä` (May / peace), `–Ϋ–Α―É―Ä―΄–Ζ` (March / Nauryz holiday). **World_core curation takes precedence** βÄî `tool_026: “¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ IsA ―΄–¥―΄―¹` is unaffected (world_core loader bypasses pattern matchers). Any text-pack extraction of these homographs as IsA subjects is dropped; the cost is a handful of rare correct extractions in exchange for eliminating an entire noise class.

### Measured delta

Full re-extract on T4_200k (`--bench-order --max-total 200000`), re-run reasoner + graph:

| | v4.0.9 | v4.0.10 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 850 | **13 787** | **βà£63** |
| extracted (Grammar) | 13 102 | 13 039 | βà£63 |
| curated (HumanApproved) | 748 | 748 | 0 |
| `is_a` facts | 659 | **623** | **βà£36** (primary target) |
| `does_to` facts | 9 192 | 9 171 | βà£21 |
| `goes_to` facts | 1 597 | 1 590 | βà£7 |
| `lives_in` facts | 289 | 288 | βà£1 |
| Other predicates | unchanged | unchanged | 0 |
| **derivations total** | **12 849** | **12 492** | **βà£357 (βà£2.8 %)** |
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 449 | **426** | βà£23 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 474 | **436** | βà£38 |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 26 | 26 | 0 |
| **R5_shared_is_a_target** | 10 827 | **10 537** | **βà£290** |
| R6_lives_in_via_part_of | 36 | 36 | 0 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 303 | 297 | βà£6 |
| R8_after_transitivity | 734 | 734 | 0 |
| Graph nodes | 3 375 | 3 374 | βà£1 |
| Graph edges | 12 449 | 12 394 | βà£55 |

### Bonus multi-matcher propagation

Because `is_time_noun` is also applied in `locative_lives_in`, `dative_goes_to`, and `agent_verb` subject filters, expanding the set with months + days tightened **all four** matchers simultaneously. The v4.0.10 diff-in-one-function produced βà£36 IsA (the explicit target) **plus** βà£29 across the other three matchers (does_to βà£21, goes_to βà£7, lives_in βà£1) βÄî 29 "free" precision wins the audit hadn't predicted. Noise leverage: **63 base facts eliminated βÜ£ 357 derivations eliminated = 5.7 derivations per base fact**.

### Visible confirmation

Most-connected content nouns on the graph rotated: v4.0.9 had ¬Ϊ–Ε―΄–Μ (151)¬Μ in the top-5 βÄî the January/February/2011 noise that made "year" artificially central. v4.0.10 drops ¬Ϊ–Ε―΄–Μ¬Μ entirely from the top-5 and promotes ¬Ϊ–Α―² (horse, degree 148)¬Μ in its place. The fix is observable in graph-level centrality, not just aggregate counts.

### Tests

**465 passing** (+2 from v4.0.9).

### Scope discipline

One concern: close the last time-noun extractor gap. No new predicates, no new rules, no data changes, no schema changes. The fix is a ~35-line diff in one function.

**Not in scope** for v4.0.10 (queued for future audits):
- **Proper-name homograph noise** βÄî ¬Ϊ–Α–±–Α–Ι IsA –Α―É―΄–Μ¬Μ (19 times βÄî there are many villages named Abai), ¬Ϊ“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ IsA “¦–Α–Μ–Α¬Μ (city in Azerbaijan). These are factually correct but collide with famous-referent senses (Abai the poet, Kazakh the people). Needs a dialog-layer sense-disambiguation pass, not an extractor guard.
- **Metaphorical proverbs** βÄî ¬Ϊ–ï“Θ–±–Β–Κ βÄî ―²–Α–±―΄―¹―²―΄“Θ “¦–Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α―Ä―΄¬Μ βÜ£ `–Β“Θ–±–Β–Κ IsA “¦–Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α―Ä`. FST extraction is structurally correct; the metaphor is lost only at the semantic level. Addressing this would need a metaphor detector (out of scope for patch-size work).

---

## [4.0.9] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî World Core batch: `plants.jsonl` + `professions.jsonl` + `tools_household.jsonl` (first fast-path batch release)

First release to exploit the v4.0.8 fast-path. Three new domains added in one patch; full data pipeline rebuild took <3 seconds instead of ~45 minutes under the old per-domain workflow. At the user's direction ("–Ϋ–Β–Ψ–±―Ö–Ψ–¥–Η–Φ–Ψ –¥–Ψ–±–Α–≤–Μ―è―²―¨ –Ψ―² ―²―Ä–Β―Ö –¥–Ψ –Ω―è―²–Η, ―΅―²–Ψ–±―΄ –≤―¹–Β ―¹―Ä–Α–Ζ―É ―²–Β―¹―²–Η―Ä–Ψ–≤–Α―²―¨"), this lands the first multi-domain batch βÄî targeting gap-fill + highest-leverage hubs.

### Three new domains βÄî rationale per domain

1. **`plants.jsonl`** (35 entries / 35 facts) βÄî **symmetry gap filler**. The v4.0.6 World Core had `animals.jsonl` (40 entries) but no flora counterpart. Adds `–Α“™–Α―à` children (“¦–Α–Ι―΄“Θ, –Β–Φ–Β–Ϋ, ―²–Β―Ä–Β–Κ, ―²–Α–Μ, “¦–Α―Ä–Α“™–Α–Ι, ―à―΄―Ä―à–Α, –Α―Ä―à–Α, “·–Ι–Β“Θ–Κ―•, –Ε–Η–¥–Β), `–≥“·–Μ` children (―Ä–Α―É―à–Α–Ϋ, “¦―΄–Ζ“™–Α–Μ–¥–Α“¦, –±”ô–Ι―à–Β―à–Β–Κ, –Μ–Α–Μ–Α, “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Φ–Ω―΄―Ä), `–±“±―²–Α` sub-hub (―²–Ψ–±―΄–Μ“™―΄, –Η―²–Φ“±―Ä―΄–Ϋ), `―à”©–Ω` children (–Ε―É―¹–Α–Ϋ, “¦–Α–Φ―΄―¹), 7 new `–¥–Α“¦―΄–Μ` species (–Α―Ä–Ω–Α, ―¹“±–Μ―΄, ―²–Α―Ä―΄, –Ε“·–≥–Β―Ä―•, –Κ“·–Ϋ–±–Α“™―΄―¹, –Ζ―΄“™―΄―Ä, –Φ–Α“¦―²–Α βÄî existing `–¥–Α“¦―΄–Μ` parent in food.jsonl), and 6 `part_of` relations (–Ε–Α–Ω―΄―Ä–Α“¦/―²–Α–Φ―΄―Ä/–±“±―²–Α“¦/―¹–Α–±–Α“¦/–±“·―Ä―à―•–Κ/―²“±“¦―΄–Φ part_of –Α“™–Α―à/”©―¹―•–Φ–¥―•–Κ). Cross-domain leverage: parents `–Α“™–Α―à`/`–≥“·–Μ`/`―à”©–Ω`/`–¥–Α“¦―΄–Μ` already in biology_basic + food, so each new child immediately gets R1 transitivity (e.g. `“¦–Α–Ι―΄“Θ βÜ£ –Α“™–Α―à βÜ£ ”©―¹―•–Φ–¥―•–Κ βÜ£ ―²―•―Ä―à―•–Μ―•–Κ –Η–Β―¹―•`) and R5 shared-IsA at both levels.

2. **`professions.jsonl`** (40 entries / 40 facts) βÄî **highest-leverage R5 hub**. Pre-v4.0.9 `–Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ` hub had ~10 children scattered across transport (–Ω–Η–Μ–Ψ―², –Κ–Α–Ω–Η―²–Α–Ϋ, –Φ–Α―à–Η–Ϋ–Η―¹―², –Ε“·―Ä–≥―•–Ζ―É―à―•), clothing (–Ζ–Β―Ä–≥–Β―Ä, ―²―•–≥―•–Ϋ―à―•), kz_literature (–Α“¦―΄–Ϋ, –Ε–Α–Ζ―É―à―΄), proverbs (“±―¹―²–Α–Ζ). Added **40 new professions** all native Kazakh: –Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ, –Ψ“¦―΄―²―É―à―΄, ―²”ô―Ä–±–Η–Β―à―•, –¥”ô―Ä―•–≥–Β―Ä, –Φ–Β–¥–±–Η–Κ–Β, “™–Α–Μ―΄–Φ, ―¹―É―Ä–Β―²―à―•, ―¹–Α–Ζ–≥–Β―Ä, ”ô–Ϋ―à―•, –±–Η―à―•, –Κ“·–Ι―à―•, –Ε―΄―Ä―à―΄, ―¹–Α―²―É―à―΄, ―¹–Α―É–¥–Α–≥–Β―Ä, –Α―¹–Ω–Α–Ζ, –Ϋ–Α―É–±–Α–Ι―à―΄, –Β–≥―•–Ϋ―à―•, –Φ–Α–Μ―à―΄, ―à–Ψ–Ω–Α–Ϋ, –Ε―΄–Μ“¦―΄―à―΄, –Α“Θ―à―΄, –±–Α–Μ―΄“¦―à―΄, “±―¹―²–Α, –Β―²―•–Κ―à―•, –¥–Α―Ä“¦–Α–Ϋ, –±–Α“¦–±–Α–Ϋ, “¦“±―Ä―΄–Μ―΄―¹―à―΄, –Ε“±–Φ―΄―¹―à―΄, –Ε”©–Ϋ–¥–Β―É―à―•, –Ζ–Α“Θ–≥–Β―Ä, ―Ö–Α―²―à―΄, ―²”©―Ä–Α“™–Α, –±–Α―¹―²―΄“¦, ―¹–Α―Ä–±–Α–Ζ, –Ε–Α―É―΄–Ϋ–≥–Β―Ä, ―²―•–Μ–Φ–Α―à, –Α―É–¥–Α―Ä–Φ–Α―à―΄, ―²―•–Μ―à―•, –Β–Φ―à―•, –Ε–Α―²―²―΄“¦―²―΄―Ä―É―à―΄. Avoided loanwords (–Φ–Β–Ϋ–Β–¥–Ε–Β―Ä, –Η–Ϋ–Ε–Β–Ϋ–Β―Ä, –Ω―Ä–Ψ–≥―Ä–Α–Φ–Φ–Η―¹―², –Α–Κ―²–Β―Ä βÄî all skipped). **Expected R5 leverage**: –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ hub now has ~50 children βÜ£ C(50,2) = 1 225 RelatedTo pairs vs pre-batch C(10,2) = 45, **27Ο½ increase**.

3. **`tools_household.jsonl`** (30 entries / 30 facts) βÄî **“¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ + ―΄–¥―΄―¹ dual hub**. `“¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ` hub children: 18 concrete tools (–Ω―΄―à–Α“¦, –±–Α–Μ―²–Α, –±–Α–Μ“™–Α, –Α―Ä–Α, “¦–Α–Ι―à―΄, –Η–Ϋ–Β, –Κ“·―Ä–Β–Κ, ―²―΄―Ä–Φ–Α, –Ψ―Ä–Α“¦, ―à–Α–Μ“™―΄, –Α―Ä“¦–Α–Ϋ, “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Φ, “¦–Α―Ä―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α―à, –¥”ô–Ω―²–Β―Ä, ―¹–Α–±―΄–Ϋ, ―à“·–±–Β―Ä–Β–Κ, ―¹―΄–Ω―΄―Ä“™―΄―à, –Ω―•―¹–Ω–Β–Κ). New `―΄–¥―΄―¹` sub-hub under “¦“±―Ä–Α–Μ: 11 vessels (―²–Α–±–Α“¦, –Κ–Β―¹–Β, “¦–Α―¹―΄“¦, ―à–Α–Ϋ―΄―à“¦―΄, ―à”ô–Ι–Ϋ–Β–Κ, ―¹–Α–Φ–Α―É―΄―Ä, “¦–Α–Ζ–Α–Ϋ, “¦“±–Φ―΄―Ä–Α, ―à–Β–Μ–Β–Κ, –Ψ–Ε–Α―É, ―²–Ψ―¹―²–Α“™–Α–Ϋ). `–Ω―•―¹–Ω–Β–Κ` and `―²–Ψ―Ä―¹―΄“¦`-style traditional Kazakh items included to keep the domain culturally grounded.

Concentrating on the –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ hub paid off: R5 shared-IsA alone gained **+4 887** (the dense profession cluster cross-chaining with existing ―²―Ä–Α–Ϋ―¹–Ω–Ψ―Ä―² / –Α―É―΄–Μ―à–Α―Ä―É–Α―à―΄–Μ―΄“¦ / –Ψ–±―Ä–Α–Ζ clusters). v4.0.7 had no equivalent hub concentration βÄî 42 transport entries spread across 3 sub-clusters (vehicles, infrastructure, professions) each gave ~C(13,2) at most.

Pre-v4.0.8 equivalent workflow (3Ο½ per-domain patches, full extract each):
- 3 Ο½ (45 min extract + 2 s reasoner + <1 s graph) = **~135 min**

**~2 000Ο½ pipeline speedup on a 3-domain batch**. Confirms the v4.0.8 infrastructure thesis empirically.

Three domains, one patch, one coherent direction (expand curated knowledge). No code changes βÄî purely additive data. 463 tests pass unchanged. Next v4.0.10: could batch another 3 domains (music_kz, sports, education are the queued candidates) βÄî or rotate axes back to rules / noise-elimination depending on where Codex review surfaces the highest-value target next.

## [4.0.8] βÄî 2026-04-24 βÄî `extract_facts --world-core-only` fast-path (throughput infrastructure)

Axis rotation toward **tooling throughput**. The previous five patches (v4.0.3 βÜ£ v4.0.7) spent ~2 hours each, of which ~45 min was a full re-extract over 200 k text samples that produced the same text-facts every time and only differed in the `world_core/` slice. At the user's explicit concern ("―²―Ä–Α―²–Η―²―¨ 2 ―΅–Α―¹–Α –Ϋ–Α –Ψ–¥–Η–Ϋ –Ω–Α―²―΅ ―¹–Η–Μ―¨–Ϋ–Ψ ―Ä–Α―¹―²–Ψ―΅–Η―²–Β–Μ―¨–Ϋ–Ψ"), v4.0.8 lands a one-time infrastructure patch that turns that 45-minute step into a ~1-second re-merge for any world_core-only change.

### Design

New `--world-core-only` flag on `extract_facts`:

1. Read the committed `data/retrieval/facts.json`.
2. `retain` every fact whose `source.pack` does **not** start with `world_core/`.
3. Re-load `data/world_core/*.jsonl` via the existing `load_world_core_facts` loader.
4. Merge the fresh curated facts, recompute `by_predicate` / `by_pack` / `facts_total` from scratch.
5. Stamp `version = CARGO_PKG_VERSION`, `status = "world_core_refresh"` (new sentinel value βÄî downstream consumers treat any `status` as first-class per the v3.1.0 iteration contract), rewrite.

Text-extraction state (`built_from`, `packs_completed`, `packs_total`, `samples_scanned`, `samples_with_facts`) is preserved verbatim βÄî the fast-path makes no claim about the text corpus, so it inherits those fields from the source artifact. A regression to those numbers requires a full `extract_facts` run (with `--bench-order --max-total 200000` for the canonical T4_200k tier).

Mutually exclusive with `--full`, `--bench-order`, `--max-total` βÄî the binary fails fast if combined.

### Measured equivalence

Baseline: committed `facts.json` @ v4.0.7 (status `"completed"`, 2 476 s elapsed). Ran `--world-core-only` and diffed byte-for-byte:

```
diff /tmp/facts_baseline.json data/retrieval/facts.json
3,4c3,4
<   "status": "completed",
<   "elapsed_s": 2476,
---
>   "status": "world_core_refresh",
>   "elapsed_s": 0,
```

**Only `status` + `elapsed_s` differ** βÄî both intentional markers. Every one of 13 745 facts, every `by_predicate` / `by_pack` count, every source chain, byte-identical. The fast-path is provably equivalent to a full re-extract when only `data/world_core/*.jsonl` has changed.

### Measured throughput win

| | full extract | fast-path |
|---|---:|---:|
| wall-clock (M2, release) | ~41 min (2 476 s) | **<1 s** (2.5 s including cargo startup) |
| text packs scanned | 9 (6 completed under the 200 k cap) | 0 |
| FST parses | ~3 M | 0 |

**~1 500Ο½ speedup** on the dominant cost of a world_core-only patch. The next 3βÄ™4 curated-knowledge patches alone recoup the ~30 min invested in this infrastructure change.

### Impact on release rhythm

Data-only patches (the axis rotation tracked in `project_v4_direction`: `world_core`, `domains`) drop from ~2 h βÜ£ ~30 min end-to-end βÄî cargo test + bump + docs + tag become the dominant cost, not extraction. This unblocks the "batch 3βÄ™5 domains per patch" direction the user flagged at v4.0.7: with the fast-path in place, adding 5 domains now rebuilds in seconds instead of 4 Ο½ 45 min = 3 h of serial re-extraction.

### Scope discipline

Single-concern patch: one new flag, one new helper function, zero changes to extractor logic, zero new predicates, zero test-count change (463 passing, unchanged from v4.0.7 βÄî correctness baseline preserved). Exactly the one-concern-per-patch rhythm the v4.x cadence was set up for.

**Guardrail**: the fast-path is **only** correct when text-extraction output is unchanged. Any patch that touches pattern matchers, the lexicon, or the corpus MUST still run a full extract. This is documented in the binary's help output and the `status = "world_core_refresh"` sentinel makes the provenance trivially greppable.

## [4.0.7] βÄî 2026-04-23 βÄî World Core expansion: new `transport.jsonl` domain

Axis rotation. Two consecutive patches (v4.0.5, v4.0.6) cleaned noise; time to grow clean knowledge. v4.0.7 adds a **14th World Core domain** βÄî `transport.jsonl` βÄî along the "domains" axis of the knowledge-first direction (`project_v4_direction`).

### New domain βÄî `transport.jsonl`

**42 entries / 42 facts**. Classic transport taxonomy centred on the `–Κ”©–Μ―•–Κ` (transport / vehicle) hub:

- **Vehicle hierarchy** (13 direct IsA –Κ”©–Μ―•–Κ): –Φ–Α―à–Η–Ϋ–Α, –Α–≤―²–Ψ–Φ–Ψ–±–Η–Μ―¨, –Ω–Ψ–Ι―΄–Ζ, “±―à–Α“¦, –Κ–Β–Φ–Β, –≤–Β–Μ–Ψ―¹–Η–Ω–Β–¥, –Φ–Ψ―²–Ψ―Ü–Η–Κ–Μ, –Α–≤―²–Ψ–±―É―¹, ―²―Ä–Α–Φ–≤–Α–Ι, –Φ–Β―²―Ä–Ψ, ―²―Ä–Ψ–Μ–Μ–Β–Ι–±―É―¹, ―²–Α–Κ―¹–Η, ―²―•–Κ“±―à–Α“¦, –Ε“·–Κ –Φ–Α―à–Η–Ϋ–Α―¹―΄.
- **Infrastructure**: –Ε–Ψ–Μ + subclasses (–Κ”©―à–Β, –¥–Α“Θ“™―΄–Μ, ―²–Α―¹ –Ε–Ψ–Μ, ―²–Β–Φ―•―Ä –Ε–Ψ–Μ, –Κ”©–Ω―•―Ä), facilities (–Α―ç―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Ψ―Ä―², –≤–Ψ–Κ–Ζ–Α–Μ, –Ω–Ψ―Ä―², –Α―è–Μ–¥–Α–Φ–Α, –±–Β–Κ–Β―²).
- **Professions**: –Ε“·―Ä–≥―•–Ζ―É―à―•, –Ω–Η–Μ–Ψ―², –Κ–Α–Ω–Η―²–Α–Ϋ, –Φ–Α―à–Η–Ϋ–Η―¹―², –Ε–Ψ–Μ–Α―É―à―΄.
- **Substances / parts**: –Ψ―²―΄–Ϋ (+ –±–Β–Ϋ–Ζ–Η–Ϋ, –¥–Η–Ζ–Β–Μ―¨, –Κ–Β―Ä–Ψ―¹–Η–Ϋ), –¥”©“Θ–≥–Β–Μ–Β–Κ, –Φ–Ψ―²–Ψ―Ä.
- **Actions / events**: “¦–Ψ–Ζ“™–Α–Μ―΄―¹, –Κ–Β–Φ–Β βÜ£ ―²–Β“Θ―•–Ζ, “±―à–Α“¦ βÜ£ –Α―ç―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Ψ―Ä―².

### Shared-IsA leverage

The 13 vehicles as direct children of `–Κ”©–Μ―•–Κ` give R5 shared-IsA up to **C(13,2) = 78** RelatedTo pairs on one hub alone. Professions cluster (4 direct ma–Φ–Α–Ϋ children) βÜ£ C(4,2)=6 more. Road hierarchy gives subclass R1 transitivity through ¬Ϊ–Κ”©―à–Β IsA –Ε–Ψ–Μ¬Μ, ¬Ϊ–¥–Α“Θ“™―΄–Μ IsA –Κ”©―à–Β¬Μ, ¬Ϊ―²–Β–Φ―•―Ä –Ε–Ψ–Μ IsA –Ε–Ψ–Μ¬Μ, etc.

The patch adds 42 curated facts and produces **+555 rule derivations** βÄî a net-effective knowledge-growth ratio of ~13Ο½ per added fact through the reasoner's cross-chain multiplier. ## [4.0.6] βÄî 2026-04-23 βÄî Narrow attributive blocklist in `is_closed_class`

Continuing the noise-elimination axis from v4.0.5. That patch shipped the **rightmost-subject** fix in `temporal_after`; spot-check then surfaced a distinct noise class the rightmost scan couldn't catch: attributive `-–Μ―΄“¦ / -–Μ―•–Κ / -–Η` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as bare nouns. When the real NP head got consumed in the ablative slot, the attributive modifier was the *only* remaining nominative candidate before the postposition βÄî so both left-to-right and right-to-left scans picked it.

### Fix

Narrow blocklist added directly to `is_closed_class`. Nine roots, each spotted on the committed v4.0.5 runtime:

| root | gloss | v4.0.5 After-fact count |
|---|---|---:|
| `–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ` | worldwide | 41 |
| `“±–Ζ–Α“¦` | long (duration) | 9 |
| `”ô―¹–Κ–Β―Ä–Η` | military | 6 |
| `―è–¥―Ä–Ψ–Μ―΄“¦` | nuclear | 3 |
| `―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦` | tropical | 2 |
| `–Ε―΄–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄“¦` | sexual / gender | 2 |
| `–Ε–Α―Ä―²` | truncated stem of ¬Ϊ–Ε–Α―Ä―²―΄¬Μ (half) | 3 |
| `–Α―Ä–Α―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α“™` | possessive-locative fragment | 4 |
| `―²–Α“™―΄` | "again / also" (adverb tagged as noun) | 3 |

Applies globally via `is_closed_class`, not just to `temporal_after`. Every pattern matcher that consults the helper (all 11) now rejects these as subjects *and* as head-noun objects in the few places where head-nouns are scanned.

### Important non-inclusions

Three roots deliberately **excluded** from the blocklist:

- `“±–Μ―²-–Α–Ζ–Α―²―²―΄“¦` (national-liberation) βÄî real compound noun; legitimate subject in some world_core / IsA contexts.
- `–±–Β–Μ–≥―•` (sign), `―¹–Α–Ϋ` (number), `–Ε“±―Ä―²` (folk) βÄî all legitimate nouns.

The regression test `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives` asserts both: the 9 blocked roots fail, and the 4 legitimate-noun roots pass through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the committed 200 k-sample runtime. All 9 attributive / fragment roots verified absent from `facts.json` as subjects (spot-checked per root: 0 occurrences each).

| | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 887 | **13 703** | **βà£184** |
| After facts | 269 | **219** | **βà£50** (primary target βÄî attributive adjectives) |
| DoesTo facts | 9 289 | 9 192 | **βà£97** (cross-matcher cleanup) |
| GoesTo facts | 1 617 | 1 595 | **βà£22** |
| LivesIn facts | 292 | 289 | βà£3 |
| RelatedTo facts | 1 467 | 1 458 | βà£9 |
| IsA facts | 525 | 524 | βà£1 |
| PartOf facts | 116 | 115 | βà£1 |
| Has facts | 226 | 225 | βà£1 |
| HasQuantity / InDomain / Causes | 40 / 24 / 22 | 40 / 24 / 22 | unchanged |

The blocklist applies globally via `is_closed_class`, so gains span every matcher that consults the helper βÄî not just `temporal_after`. The DoesTo `βà£97` and GoesTo `βà£22` drops are the attributive-as-agent cases that the Codex review didn't surface on the After side: e.g. ¬Ϊ–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ “·―Ä–¥―•―¹ X-–Ϋ―• ―²―É–¥―΄―Ä–Α–¥―΄¬Μ βÜ£ pre-v4.0.6 extracted as `(–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ, DoesTo, X)`.

Per-rule derivation deltas:

| rule | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 361 | 361 | 0 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 422 | 417 | βà£5 |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 26 | 26 | 0 |
| R5_shared_is_a_target | 5 437 | 5 437 | 0 |
| R6_lives_in_via_part_of | 36 | 36 | 0 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 297 | 300 | +3 |
| R8_after_transitivity | 714 | 734 | +20 |
| **total derivations** | **7 293** | **7 311** | **+18** |

Small R7 and R8 *increases* are structural: with fewer attributive-subjected base facts, the reasoner's `seen_triples` dedup set is smaller, so a few chains that were previously short-circuited now fire freely. The new derivations use clean content-noun subjects where the noisy attributive ones were blocked.

Graph: 3 287 βÜ£ **3 284** nodes (βà£3), 12 439 βÜ£ **12 308** edges (βà£131). Most-connected content nouns: **–Α–¥–Α–Φ (288), –Ε–Β―Ä (218), –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β (207), “¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ (201), –Ε―΄–Μ (151)**.

### Tests

**463 passing** (+1 from v4.0.5): `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives`.

### Scope discipline

One helper, nine new entries, one regression test. No rule changes, no world_core changes, no extractor-logic changes. Sequential 1βÜ£9 cadence preserved (v4.0.5 βÜ£ v4.0.6 βÜ£ v4.0.7).

### What's next (v4.0.7)

Axes continue to rotate per `project_v4_direction`:
- **World Core** expansion in an existing / new domain
- **New reasoning rule** R9 candidate
- More **noise elimination** if new classes surface
- **Corpus** βÄî long-horizon FST-synthetic data generation

## [4.0.5] βÄî 2026-04-23 βÄî Noise elimination in `temporal_after` subject selector

Continuing the v4.0.x curriculum βÄî one axis per patch, this one is **noise elimination**. Rotating axes keep new rule leverage (v4.0.4 R8) from compounding existing matcher precision gaps.

### Root cause

v4.0.4 spot-check showed R8 producing derivations like `(―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦, After, –Α–Ι―΄–Ω)` βÄî the chain was mathematically sound but inherited a noisy base fact `(―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦, After, –Ε―΄–Μ)` from `temporal_after`. Source: ¬Ϊ–ï–≥–Β―Ä **―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦** –Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥–Α―Ä –Ψ―¹―΄–Ϋ–¥–Α–Ι “¦–Α―Ä“¦―΄–Ϋ–Φ–Β–Ϋ –Ε–Ψ–Ι―΄–Μ–Α –±–Β―Ä―¹–Β, 80-40 **–Ε―΄–Μ–¥–Α–Ϋ** ―¹–Ψ“Θ –Ε–Β―Ä –±–Β―²―•–Ϋ–¥–Β –Φ“±–Ϋ–¥–Α–Ι –Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥–Α―Ä “¦–Α–Μ–Φ–Α–Ι–¥―΄¬Μ. The matcher scanned left-to-right and grabbed the first bare-nominative noun (`―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦`, an attributive modifier) as the subject, when Kazakh SOV structure places the NP head (`–Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥–Α―Ä`) closer to the verb.

### Fix

Two tiny guards in `temporal_after`:

1. **Rightmost subject, not leftmost** (`(0..post_idx-1).rev().find_map(...)` instead of `(0..post_idx-1).find_map(...)`). In Kazakh SOV the subject-NP head sits closer to the ablative / verb, so the rightmost bare-nominative candidate before the postposition is the real subject.
2. **3-char minimum root length** (mirrors the guards already present in `locative_lives_in` and `dative_goes_to`). Blocks any truncated FST stems that might leak through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the same committed 200 k-sample runtime:

| | v4.0.4 | v4.0.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 889 | **13 887** | βà£2 |
| After facts | 269 | 269 | 0 (net) |
| R8_after_transitivity | 789 | **714** | **βà£75 (βà£9.5 %)** |
| total derivations | 7 368 | **7 293** | **βà£75** |
| graph nodes | 3 286 | 3 287 | +1 |
| graph edges | 12 447 | 12 439 | βà£8 |

The rightmost-subject fix correctly narrowed the `(―²―Ä–Ψ–Ω–Η–Κ–Α–Μ―΄“¦, After, *)` class (from 2 βÜ£ 1 base facts, with R8 transitive multiplication eliminated). Most of the 75 blocked R8 derivations came from that transitive multiplication.

### Honest observation βÄî adjacent noise class identified

The spot-check surfaced a **different** noise class still active at v4.0.5: attributive `-–Μ―΄“¦ / -–Μ―•–Κ / -–Η` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as nouns. Top offender: **¬Ϊ–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ¬Μ** (worldwide) βÄî 41 `After` facts in the committed runtime, typically from patterns like ¬Ϊ–ë―•―Ä―•–Ϋ―à―• –¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ ―¹–Ψ“™―΄―¹―²–Α–Ϋ –Κ–Β–Ι―•–Ϋ...¬Μ where the REAL subject is elided (implicit event) and the grab-the-attributive heuristic still wins even with rightmost-scan because the head noun (`―¹–Ψ“™―΄―¹`) sits in the ablative slot, consumed as the object.

Also seen: `―è–¥―Ä–Ψ–Μ―΄“¦` (nuclear, Ο½3), `”ô―¹–Κ–Β―Ä–Η` (military, Ο½6), `“±–Μ―²-–Α–Ζ–Α―²―²―΄“¦` (national-liberation, Ο½3), `–Ε―΄–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄“¦` (sexual / gender, Ο½2), `“±–Ζ–Α“¦` (long, Ο½9).

Fixing this requires a different tool: a narrow **attributive blocklist** for known -–Μ―΄“¦/-–Η adjective-acting roots. Queued for the next noise-elimination patch to keep v4.0.5 single-concern per the cadence rule.

### Curated temporal chains preserved

The 6 clean seasonal / daytime R8 closures from v4.0.4 are invariant under the rightmost-scan change βÄî they pass through a single-subject-candidate path where left-to-right and right-to-left identify the same token:

| subject | After | object |
|---|---|---|
| –Κ“·–Ζ | After | –Κ”©–Κ―²–Β–Φ |
| “¦―΄―¹ | After | –Ε–Α–Ζ |
| “¦―΄―¹ | After | –Κ”©–Κ―²–Β–Φ |
| ―²“·–Ϋ | After | ―²“·―¹ |
| ―²“·–Ϋ | After | ―²–Α“Θ |
| –Κ–Β―à | After | ―²–Α“Θ |

### Tests

**462 passing** (+1 from v4.0.4): new `temporal_after_picks_rightmost_subject_not_attributive` uses `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦ ―Ö–Α–Μ―΄“¦ –Ε―΄–Μ–¥–Α–Ϋ ―¹–Ψ“Θ ”©–Ζ–≥–Β―Ä–Β–¥―•` to verify that:
- The matcher picks `―Ö–Α–Μ―΄“¦` (head of the NP), not `“¦–Α–Ζ–Α“¦` (attributive).
- Object stays `–Ε―΄–Μ` (ablative reference point).

Existing `temporal_after_extracts_noon_after_morning` continues to pass βÄî the single-subject-candidate case is invariant under direction change.

### Scope discipline

One concern per patch. Only `temporal_after` subject selector touched, no rule changes, no world_core changes. Sequential 1βÜ£9 cadence preserved (v4.0.4 βÜ£ v4.0.5 βÜ£ v4.0.6).

### What's next

Axes continue to rotate:
- **noise elimination**: narrow attributive-adjectival blocklist (`–¥“·–Ϋ–Η–Β–Ε“·–Ζ―•–Μ―•–Κ`, `―è–¥―Ä–Ψ–Μ―΄“¦`, `”ô―¹–Κ–Β―Ä–Η`, `“±–Μ―²-–Α–Ζ–Α―²―²―΄“¦`, `–Ε―΄–Ϋ―΄―¹―²―΄“¦`, `“±–Ζ–Α“¦`) βÄî would knock out ~58 base After facts + their transitive R8 multiplications. Targeted v4.0.6.
- **reasoning rules**: R9 candidate ideas βÄî After anti-symmetry curator warning (R4-style), or Causes-transitivity with type guards.
- **world_core / Lexicon**: gap `–Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Ϋ` (forest) surfaced by this patch's test authoring βÄî new entries for nature domain.
- **corpus**: FST-synthetic clean data generation remains the long-horizon axis.
