**Policy:** plain `{name}` substitution still keeps the user's original spelling ("―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ John"). Only `{name|features}` triggers transliteration βÜ£ synthesis.

### Cross-slot templates

New templates reference multiple session entities in one response. Eligible only when every slot is fillable; plain variants stay available otherwise.

| key | new templates |
|---|---|
| `ask_how_are_you` | `"–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ {name}, –Α–Μ ―¹―•–Ζ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι―¹―΄–Ζ"`, `"–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ, ―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―² {name}"` |
| `statement_of_age` | `"{name}, {age} –Ε–Α―¹―²–Α―¹―΄–Ζ, ―²–Α–Φ–Α―à–Α"`, `"{name}, {age} –Ε–Α―¹ βÄî –Κ–Β―Ä–Β–Φ–Β―² –Κ–Β–Ζ–Β“Θ"` |
| `statement_of_occupation` | `"{name}, {occupation} βÄî “¦“±―Ä–Φ–Β―²―²―• –Κ”ô―¹―•–Ω"`, `"{name}, ―¹―•–Ζ {city|locative} {occupation} –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ"` |
| `compliment` | `"―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―² {name}"`, `"―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―² {name}, ―¹―•–Ζ –¥–Β ―²–Α–Φ–Α―à–Α―¹―΄–Ζ"` |

The triple-slot `"{name}, ―¹―•–Ζ {city|locative} {occupation} –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ"` only fires after the user has stated all three entities βÄî "–î”ô―É–Μ–Β―², ―¹―•–Ζ –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α ”ô–Ϋ―à―• –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ".

### Tests

81 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 78), 3 new cross-slot tests covering (name+ask_how_are_you), (name+age), and (name+city+occupation triple). 23 lib-level unit tests (13 slot_syntax + 6 transliteration + 4 planner).

Workspace: **265 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.8 limitations

- Silent `h` in English is not special-cased (`John βÜ£ –î–Ε–Ψ―Ö–Ϋ` rather than the standard spelling `–î–Ε–Ψ–Ϋ`). Good enough for FST synthesis; a more polished transliterator is v1.0.0+ work.
- Back-vowel instrumental harmony (`–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄` βÜ£ `–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–Φ–Α–Ϋ` rather than `–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–Φ–Β–Ϋ`) is a pre-existing FST quirk βÄî check `INSTRUMENTAL` template archiphoneme `E` resolution. Out of scope for the dialog layer.
- Derivation + Possessive tokens are fully parseable; the current template set uses only a handful of the 18 feature tokens. Template authors have the full surface available when needed.

## [0.9.7] βÄî 2026-04-19

Lexicon-backed occupation recognition. The fixed 6-form table (`–Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ–Φ―•–Ϋ βÜ£ –Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ` and five others) is replaced with generic 1sg-copula stripping + noun lookup against the 14 k-entry Lexicon. Any noun in the Lexicon ending in a 1sg predicate suffix (`-–Φ―΄–Ϋ/-–Φ―•–Ϋ/-–Ω―΄–Ϋ/-–Ω―•–Ϋ/-–±―΄–Ϋ/-–±―•–Ϋ`) is now recognised.

### What now works

```
$ adam_chat
> –Φ–Β–Ϋ –Α“¦―΄–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ           βÜ£ ―¹―•–Ζ –Α“¦―΄–Ϋ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ            (new: –Α“¦―΄–Ϋ is in Lexicon, not in the old table)
> –Φ–Β–Ϋ ”ô–Ϋ―à―•–Φ―•–Ϋ           βÜ£ ”ô–Ϋ―à―•–Μ–Β―Ä βÄî “¦–Α–Ε–Β―²―²―• –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥―΄“¦  (FST plural on the new extract)
> –Φ–Β–Ϋ “™–Α–Μ―΄–Φ–Φ―΄–Ϋ          βÜ£ ―¹―•–Ζ “™–Α–Μ―΄–Φ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ
> –Φ–Β–Ϋ ―¹―É―Ä–Β―²―à―•–Φ―•–Ϋ        βÜ£ ―¹―•–Ζ ―¹―É―Ä–Β―²―à―• –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ
> –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ              βÜ£ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ                  (POS filter βÜ£ wellbeing, not occupation)
```

### Public API additions

- `adam_dialog::interpret_text_with_lexicon(input, parses, Option<&LexiconV1>) -> Intent`
- `adam_dialog::semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon` (module-level)

The original `interpret_text(input, parses)` is now a thin wrapper that calls the lexicon-aware variant with `None` βÄî existing callers keep working.

### Implementation detail

```rust
fn strip_copula_and_lookup_noun(tokens: &[String], lex: &LexiconV1) -> Option<String> {
    const COPULA_SUFFIXES: &[&str] = &["–Φ―΄–Ϋ", "–Φ―•–Ϋ", "–Ω―΄–Ϋ", "–Ω―•–Ϋ", "–±―΄–Ϋ", "–±―•–Ϋ"];
    for t in tokens {
        for suffix in COPULA_SUFFIXES {
            let Some(root) = strip_suffix_chars(t, suffix) else { continue };
            if root.chars().count() < 2 { continue; }
            if let Some(entry) = lex.get(&root) {
                if entry.part_of_speech == "noun" {
                    return Some(root);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    None
}
```

- **POS filter** rejects adjectives (`–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄`, `–Ε–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ`) so "–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ" still routes to StatementOfWellbeing.
- **Min-length 2** guards against stripping into short function words.
- **Char-count indexing** keeps UTF-8 boundaries safe.

`respond`, `respond_with_repo`, and `Conversation::turn` all pass the lexicon into the new recogniser automatically.

### Tests

78 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 73), 5 new:
- 1 positive case covering `–Α“¦―΄–Ϋ` (out-of-table noun)
- 1 bulk test for `”ô–Ϋ―à―• / “™–Α–Μ―΄–Φ / ―¹―É―Ä–Β―²―à―•`
- 1 adjective-negative-case ensuring `–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ` stays wellbeing
- 1 unknown-root case (`xyzzy–Φ―΄–Ϋ` βÜ£ must not become occupation)
- 1 multi-turn absorption test (lexicon-derived occupation persists to session)

Workspace: **251 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.7 limitations

- LatinβÜ£Cyrillic transliteration is intentionally NOT shipped. Latin names ("John") continue to bypass `{name|features}` FST synthesis via the v0.9.6 safety guard. Transliteration lands in v0.9.8 alongside broader slot-syntax enrichment.
- Lexicon coverage is the cap βÄî occupations not in the 14 k-entry Lexicon (`―³–Η–Μ–Ψ―¹–Ψ―³`, `–Ω―Ä–Ψ–≥―Ä–Α–Φ–Φ–Η―¹―²`) still don't extract. Data-layer expansion is orthogonal work.

## [0.9.6] βÄî 2026-04-19

Multilingual recogniser surface. The model now reads Kazakh, Russian, and English input across all 25 intents and replies exclusively in Kazakh. This is NOT translation βÄî the core pipeline stays deterministic Kazakh-only. The expansion is purely at the recogniser layer: more surface forms map to the same Intent taxonomy.

### Triggers added (per intent)

- **Greeting** (casual/polite/time-of-day): `hi/hello/hey`, `–Ω―Ä–Η–≤–Β―²`, `–Ζ–¥―Ä–Α–≤―¹―²–≤―É–Ι―²–Β`, `–¥–Ψ–±―Ä–Ψ–Β ―É―²―Ä–Ψ/–¥–Β–Ϋ―¨/–≤–Β―΅–Β―Ä`, `good morning/afternoon/evening/day`
- **Farewell**: `bye/goodbye/see you`, `–¥–Ψ ―¹–≤–Η–¥–Α–Ϋ–Η―è/–Ω–Ψ–Κ–Α`
- **Affirmation**: `yes/yeah/yep/sure/ok`, `–¥–Α/–Κ–Ψ–Ϋ–Β―΅–Ϋ–Ψ/–Α–≥–Α`
- **Negation**: `no/nope/nah`, `–Ϋ–Β―²`
- **Thanks**: `thanks/thank you`, `―¹–Ω–Α―¹–Η–±–Ψ/–±–Ψ–Μ―¨―à–Ψ–Β ―¹–Ω–Α―¹–Η–±–Ψ`
- **Apology**: `sorry/excuse me`, `–Η–Ζ–≤–Η–Ϋ–Η/–Η–Ζ–≤–Η–Ϋ–Η―²–Β/–Ω―Ä–Ψ―¹―²–Η`
- **AskHowAreYou**: `how are you/how's it`, `–Κ–Α–Κ –¥–Β–Μ–Α/–Κ–Α–Κ ―²―΄/–Κ–Α–Κ –≤―΄`
- **StatementOfWellbeing**: `fine/great/i'm good/i'm fine`, `―Ö–Ψ―Ä–Ψ―à–Ψ/–Ϋ–Ψ―Ä–Φ–Α–Μ―¨–Ϋ–Ψ/–Ψ―²–Μ–Η―΅–Ϋ–Ψ`
- **AskName**: `what is/what's your name`, `–Κ–Α–Κ ―²–Β–±―è/–≤–Α―¹ –Ζ–Ψ–≤―É―²`
- **StatementOfName**: four new patterns βÄî
  - `me–Ϋ―è –Ζ–Ψ–≤―É―² <N>`, `–Φ–Ψ―ë –Η–Φ―è <N>` (Russian)
  - `my name is <N>`, `call me <N>`, `hi i am <N>` (English; bare "I am X" is ambiguous so requires a leading greet token)
- **AskAge**: `how old are you`, `―¹–Κ–Ψ–Μ―¨–Κ–Ψ ―²–Β–±–Β/–≤–Α–Φ –Μ–Β―²`
- **AskLocation**: `where are you from / where do you live`, `–Ψ―²–Κ―É–¥–Α ―²―΄/–≤―΄`
- **AskOccupation**: `what do you do / what's your job`, `–Κ–Β–Φ ―Ä–Α–±–Ψ―²–Α–Β―à―¨/–Ζ–Α–Ϋ–Η–Φ–Α–Β―à―¨―¹―è`
- **AskWeather**: `how's / what's the weather`, `–Κ–Α–Κ–Α―è –Ω–Ψ–≥–Ψ–¥–Α`
- **AskTime**: `what time is it / what's the time`, `―¹–Κ–Ψ–Μ―¨–Κ–Ψ –≤―Ä–Β–Φ–Β–Ϋ–Η/–Κ–Ψ―²–Ψ―Ä―΄–Ι ―΅–Α―¹`
- **Compliment**: `great/awesome/wonderful/excellent/well done`, `–Φ–Ψ–Μ–Ψ–¥–Β―Ü/–Ψ―²–Μ–Η―΅–Ϋ–Ψ/–Ζ–¥–Ψ―Ä–Ψ–≤–Ψ`
- **Request**: `please/need help/can you help`, `–Ω–Ψ–Ε–Α–Μ―É–Ι―¹―²–Α/–Ω–Ψ–Φ–Ψ–≥–Η―²–Β/–Ω–Ψ–Φ–Ψ–≥–Η`
- **WellWishes**: `good luck/all the best`, `―É–¥–Α―΅–Η/–≤―¹–Β–≥–Ψ –Ϋ–Α–Η–Μ―É―΅―à–Β–≥–Ψ`

### Safety guard for Latin roots

FST phonology is tuned for Kazakh Cyrillic. Feeding `"John"` into `synthesise_noun(..., Case::Instrumental)` would produce garbled `"John–Φ–Α–Ϋ"`. The realiser now detects non-Cyrillic roots and falls back to plain substitution βÄî no suffix attached. Output: `"John ―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹“¦–Α–Ϋ―΄–Φ–Α “¦―É–Α–Ϋ―΄―à―²―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ"` rather than hallucinated morphology.

### Ordering change

`StatementOfName` is now checked BEFORE `Greeting` in `interpret_text`. This prevents `"hi i am John"` from misfiring as a bare Casual greeting. All StatementOfName patterns (–Α―²―΄–Φ/–Β―¹―•–Φ―•–Φ/–Ζ–Ψ–≤―É―²/my name is/call me/[greet] i am X) are explicit enough to rule out false positives.

### Tests

73 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 56), 17 new:
- 10 recogniser triggers (greetings Ο½ 3, farewell, affirmation, negation, thanks, apology, ask-how-are-you, ask-name)
- 4 self-introduction patterns (Russian `–Ζ–Ψ–≤―É―²`, English `my name is` / `call me` / `hi i am`)
- 2 output-is-Kazakh invariants (Russian input βÜ£ Cyrillic-only output; Latin name βÜ£ no FST suffix)
- 1 multilingual multi-turn conversation flow

Workspace: **245 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.6 limitations

- Recogniser catches the common phrasings. Edge cases (British contractions "init", ru-ua mix, Kazakh with Latin transliteration "salem") are not handled.
- Latin names stay un-inflected in templates requesting `{name|features}`. Transliteration to Cyrillic (e.g. `John` βÜ£ `–î–Ε–Ψ–Ϋ`) would let the FST synthesise properly βÄî possible future work.
- No output-language switching: Russian / English input still gets Kazakh output by design.

## [0.9.5] βÄî 2026-04-19

FST-backed slot expansion. Templates can now emit `{slot|features}` atoms; the realiser synthesises grammatical forms via `adam_kernel_fst::morphotactics::synthesise_noun` instead of plain text substitution. Cross-slot templates (using multiple slots in one response) drop in naturally because of the v0.8.5 template-fillability filter.

### New slot syntax

```text
{slot}                    βÄî plain: substitute slot value verbatim
{slot|feat1+feat2+...}    βÄî FST: synthesise via morphotactics
```

Feature tokens (case-insensitive, `+`-separated, unknown tokens ignored):

| token | βÜ£ field |
|---|---|
| `nominative / nom` | `case = Nominative` |
| `genitive / gen` | `case = Genitive` |
| `dative / dat` | `case = Dative` |
| `accusative / acc` | `case = Accusative` |
| `locative / loc` | `case = Locative` |
| `ablative / abl` | `case = Ablative` |
| `instrumental / inst` | `case = Instrumental` |
| `singular / sg` | `number = Singular` |
| `plural / pl` | `number = Plural` |

### Examples of what now works

| template | filled | rendered |
|---|---|---|
| `{city\|locative} ―²“±―Ä–Α―¹―΄–Ζ –±–Α` | city=–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ | –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α ―²“±―Ä–Α―¹―΄–Ζ –±–Α |
| `{city\|ablative} ―Ö–Α–±–Α―Ä –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Φ–Α` | city=–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ | –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α–Ϋ ―Ö–Α–±–Α―Ä –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Φ–Α |
| `{name\|instrumental} ―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹“¦–Α–Ϋ―΄–Φ–Α “¦―É–Α–Ϋ―΄―à―²―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ` | name=–î”ô―É–Μ–Β―² | –î”ô―É–Μ–Β―²–Ω–Β–Ϋ ―²–Α–Ϋ―΄―¹“¦–Α–Ϋ―΄–Φ–Α “¦―É–Α–Ϋ―΄―à―²―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ |
| `{occupation\|plural} βÄî “¦–Α–Ε–Β―²―²―• –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥―΄“¦` | occupation=–Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ | –Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ–¥–Β―Ä βÄî “¦–Α–Ε–Β―²―²―• –Φ–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ–¥―΄“¦ |
| `―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ {name}, {city\|ablative} ―Ö–Α–±–Α―Ä –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Φ–Α` | name=–î”ô―É–Μ–Β―², city=–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄ | ―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ –î”ô―É–Μ–Β―², –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α–Ϋ ―Ö–Α–±–Α―Ä –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Φ–Α |

The last one is a cross-slot template: the planner only considers it when BOTH `name` and `city` are in session.

### Public API additions

- `adam_dialog::slot_syntax::{parse_placeholder, parse_noun_features}`

### TOML changes (v1.toml version βÜ£ 0.9.5)

- `greeting.casual`, `greeting.polite` each get a cross-slot `{name}+{city|abl/loc}` variant.
- `statement_of_name` gets `{name|instrumental}` variants.
- `statement_of_location` gets 3 FST-backed variants: locative / ablative / dative.
- `statement_of_occupation` gets plural + dative variants.

### Tests

56 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 52), 4 new covering every FST-backed expansion path + the cross-slot greeting. 7 slot-syntax unit tests + 1 additional planner unit test. 1 doc-test.

Workspace: **229 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

### Known v0.9.5 limitations

- Feature parser covers noun `case + number` only. Derivation and possessive are v1.0.0 additions.
- Occupation recogniser still uses the fixed 6-form table; generic 1sg-copula stripping via FST lookup is queued for v0.9.8.
- No verb slot expansion βÄî `{root|verb_features}` would need a different synthesiser dispatch.

## [0.9.0] βÄî 2026-04-19

Full entity absorption: every social-topic statement now contributes an extractable entity to session state. Age is parsed from Kazakh numerals (1βÄ™99), city from ablative/locative case stripping, occupation from 1sg-copula stripping.

### Intent payload changes (breaking)

- `StatementOfAge` βÜ£ `StatementOfAge { years: Option<u32> }`
- `StatementOfLocation` βÜ£ `StatementOfLocation { city: Option<String> }`
- `StatementOfOccupation` βÜ£ `StatementOfOccupation { occupation: Option<String> }`

`None` means the intent matched on keywords but the entity wasn't parseable ("–Ε–Α―¹―΄–Φ –Ε–Α―¹―΄―Ä―΄–Ϋ").

### Numeral parser

`semantics::parse_kazakh_age` handles:

- Bare tens: –Ψ–Ϋ (10), –Ε–Η―΄―Ä–Φ–Α (20), –Ψ―²―΄–Ζ (30), “¦―΄―Ä―΄“¦ (40), –Β–Μ―É (50), –Α–Μ–Ω―΄―¹ (60), –Ε–Β―²–Ω―•―¹ (70), ―¹–Β–Κ―¹–Β–Ϋ (80), ―²–Ψ“¦―¹–Α–Ϋ (90)
- Bare units: –±―•―Ä (1) βÄΠ ―²–Ψ“™―΄–Ζ (9)
- Compound forms: "–Ψ―²―΄–Ζ –±–Β―¹" (35), "–Ε–Η―΄―Ä–Φ–Α –Β–Κ―•" (22)
- Literal digit strings: "30"

### Entity extraction

- `StatementOfLocation`: strips ablative+copula (`-–¥–Α–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ/-–¥–Β–Ϋ–Φ―•–Ϋ/-―²–Α–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ/-―²–Β–Ϋ–Φ―•–Ϋ`) or locative (`-–¥–Α/-–¥–Β/-―²–Α/-―²–Β`) to recover the city root. Preserves original casing: "–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ" βÜ£ "–ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄"; "–Α―¹―²–Α–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α ―²“±―Ä–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ" βÜ£ "–Α―¹―²–Α–Ϋ–Α".
- `StatementOfOccupation`: matches a fixed table of 1sg-copula forms and emits the stripped noun root: "–Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ–Φ―•–Ϋ" βÜ£ "–Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ".

### Session wiring

- `Conversation::absorb_entities` and `planner::extract_slots` both consume the new fields and populate `{age}`, `{city}`, `{occupation}` slots (in addition to `{name}` from v0.8.5).
- Once absorbed, the entities persist across turns just like `{name}` does.

### Templates (TOML v0.9.0)

New personalised variants in `statement_of_age`, `statement_of_location`, `statement_of_occupation`:

- `statement_of_age`: `"{age} –Ε–Α―¹ βÄî ―²–Α–Φ–Α―à–Α –Κ–Β–Ζ–Β“Θ"`, `"–Ε–Α―¹―΄“Θ―΄–Ζ {age} –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ"`
- `statement_of_location`: `"{city} βÄî ”ô–¥–Β–Φ―• “¦–Α–Μ–Α"`, `"{city} ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Κ”©–Ω –Β―¹―²―•–¥―•–Φ"`
- `statement_of_occupation`: `"{occupation} βÄî “¦“±―Ä–Φ–Β―²―²―• –Κ”ô―¹―•–Ω"`, `"―¹―•–Ζ {occupation} –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ―¹―•–Ζ"`

Only eligible when the slot can be filled; untouched by templates stay canonical for utterances without extractable entities.

### Tests

52 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 44), 8 new:

- 3 intent tests covering age numeral parsing (bare, compound, none)
- 2 location extraction tests (ablative + locative)
- 1 occupation extraction test
- 1 multi-turn absorption test (age+city+occupation into session)
- 1 multi-turn personalisation test (numeral appears in response)

Workspace: **215 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

### Known v0.9.0 limitations

- Occupation extraction uses a fixed 6-form table. Regular 1sg-copula stripping via FST lookup lands in v0.9.5 together with `{root|features}` slot expansion.
- Location extraction is surface-pattern only βÄî no FST lookup yet, so misspelt or inflected cities ("“ö―΄–Ζ―΄–Μ–Ψ―Ä–¥–Α–¥–Α–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ") get a raw root rather than normalised lexicon lemma.
- No cross-slot templates yet (`"{name}, ―¹―•–Ζ {age} –Ε–Α―¹―²–Α―¹―΄–Ζ –±–Α?"` βÄî possible but unwritten).

## [0.8.5] βÄî 2026-04-19

First session state in the dialog layer. The new [`Conversation`] struct accumulates entities across turns, so a user who introduces themselves once gets greeted by name on every subsequent turn.

- `Conversation { session: HashMap<String, String> }` with `new()`, `turn(input, lex, repo, seed) -> String`, `reset()`.
- `planner::plan_response_with_session(intent, seed, repo, session)` merges per-turn slots with session slots (per-turn wins on collision) and filters candidate templates down to those whose every `{slot}` reference is satisfiable. If filtering empties the pool, falls back to the full pool (visible `{slot}` is better than a crash).
- `plan_response_with_repo` is now defined in terms of `plan_response_with_session(βÄΠ, &HashMap::new())` βÄî no behaviour change for existing callers.
- Greeting families get `{name}` variants:
  - `greeting.casual`: ―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ / ―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ –¥–Ψ―¹―²―΄–Φ / **―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ {name}**
  - `greeting.polite`: ―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ–Β―²―¹―•–Ζ –±–Β / –Α―Ä–Φ―΄―¹―΄–Ζ / **―¹”ô–Μ–Β–Φ–Β―²―¹―•–Ζ –±–Β {name}**
  - `greeting.morning` / `.day` / `.evening` all get a corresponding `{name}` variant.
- `adam_chat` CLI now holds a single `Conversation` for the whole REPL session; `--trace` mode dumps the live session map.
- Ordering: `Conversation::turn` absorbs entities BEFORE planning, so the SAME turn that says "–Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –Α―²―΄–Φ X" can already receive a response containing `{name}` substituted to X.

Tests: 44 dialog end-to-end pairs (+3 session tests covering persistence, non-persistence when no name said, and `reset()`). 3 planner unit tests for `template_is_fillable`. Workspace: **204 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.8.5 limitations:

- Only `name` is persisted across turns βÄî `age`, `location`, `occupation`, `family` recognition exists but their entities aren't yet extracted into session. That lands in v0.9.0 together with numeric extraction.
- No context-aware responses: the model doesn't say "–Φ–Β–Ϋ ―¹―•–Ζ–¥―• –î”ô―É–Μ–Β―² –¥–Β–Ω –Α―²–Α–¥―΄–Φ, –Η”ô?" to confirm, or disambiguate "–î”ô―É–Μ–Β―²" the name from "–¥”ô―É–Μ–Β―²" the concept.

## [0.8.0] βÄî 2026-04-19

Dialog layer widened from 10 to **25 intents**. First entity extraction lands: the user's name is pulled out of self-introduction patterns and substituted into the response template.

New intents (+15, recognisers in `semantics.rs`):

- `StatementOfName { name }` βÄî "–Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –Α―²―΄–Φ X" / "–Φ–Β–Ϋ―• X –¥–Β–Ω –Α―²–Α–Ι–¥―΄" / "–Β―¹―•–Φ―•–Φ X"
- `AskAge` / `StatementOfAge` βÄî –Ε–Α―¹―΄“Θ –Ϋ–Β―à–Β / –Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –Ε–Α―¹―΄–Φ –Ψ―²―΄–Ζ
- `AskLocation` / `StatementOfLocation` βÄî “¦–Α–Ι–¥–Α ―²“±―Ä–Α―¹―΄–Ζ / –Φ–Β–Ϋ –ê–Μ–Φ–Α―²―΄–¥–Α–Ϋ–Φ―΄–Ϋ
- `AskOccupation` / `StatementOfOccupation` βÄî –Ϋ–Β–Φ–Β–Ϋ –Α–Ι–Ϋ–Α–Μ―΄―¹–Α―¹―΄“Θ / –Φ–Β–Ϋ –Φ“±“™–Α–Μ―•–Φ–Φ―•–Ϋ
- `AskFamily` / `StatementOfFamily` βÄî –±–Α–Μ–Α–Μ–Α―Ä―΄“Θ –±–Α―Ä –Φ–Α / –Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –±–Α–Μ–Α–Φ –±–Α―Ä
- `AskWeather` / `StatementOfWeather` βÄî –Α―É–Α ―Ä–Α–Ι―΄ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι / –±“·–≥―•–Ϋ ―¹―É―΄“¦
- `AskTime` βÄî ―¹–Α“™–Α―² –Ϋ–Β―à–Β
- `Compliment` βÄî –Ε–Α―Ä–Α–Ι―¹―΄“Θ / –Κ–Β―Ä–Β–Φ–Β―² / ―²–Α–Φ–Α―à–Α
- `Request` βÄî ”©―²―•–Ϋ–Β–Φ―•–Ϋ / –Κ”©–Φ–Β–Κ―²–Β―¹―•“Θ―•–Ζ―à―•
- `WellWishes` βÄî ―¹”ô―²―²―•–Μ―•–Κ / –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Κ“·–Ϋ ―²―•–Μ–Β–Ι–Φ―•–Ϋ

Entity extraction + slot expansion:

- `semantics::detect_statement_of_name` extracts the PersonName from three surface patterns (–Α―²―΄–Φ / –Φ–Β–Ϋ―• X –¥–Β–Ω –Α―²–Α–Ι–¥―΄ / –Β―¹―•–Φ―•–Φ) with case-preserving capitalisation.
- `ResponsePlan` gains a `slots: HashMap<String, String>` field populated by the planner from the Intent.
- `realiser::realise` substitutes `{slot}` placeholders in the chosen template; templates like `"“¦–Ψ―à –Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•“Θ―•–Ζ {name}"` now personalise.

Ordering subtlety: Statement-of-X is checked BEFORE Ask-of-X in every topic pair βÄî a 1st-person marker ("–Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•–Φ", "―²“±―Ä–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ", "–Ε–Α―¹―΄–Φ") unambiguously identifies the user as stating, not asking. Without this, "“¦–Α–Ι–¥–Α–Ϋ –Κ–Β–Μ–¥―•–Φ" would hit `AskLocation` first (because of "“¦–Α–Ι–¥–Α–Ϋ").

TOML repository: +15 families βÜ£ 29 families total, version = "0.8.0".

Tests: 41 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 23), 18 new covering recognition, slot substitution, and planner coverage for every new intent. Workspace: **201 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.8.0 limitations (by design, not bugs):

- No session state: the model doesn't remember the user's name across turns. Adding a `Conversation` struct lands in v0.8.5.
- Numeric extraction (age, time) is a v0.9.0 concern; StatementOfAge templates acknowledge generically.
- Templates are still literal phrases with optional `{slot}` text replacement. FST-backed `{root|features}` atoms land in v0.9.0.
- Templates have not been native-speaker reviewed βÄî stiffness is expected; v0.9.0 tightens phrasing.

## [0.7.5] βÄî 2026-04-19

Dialog layer widened from 5 to **10 intents** and templates externalised to TOML.

New intents (+recognisers in `semantics.rs`):

- `Thanks` βÄî ―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―² / –Κ”©–Ω ―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―² / ―Ä–Α“¦–Φ–Β―² βÜ£ –Ψ“¦–Α―¹―΄ –Ε–Ψ“¦, –Β―à―²–Β“Θ–Β –Β–Φ–Β―¹, ―Ä–Η–Ζ–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ
- `Apology` βÄî –Κ–Β―à―•―Ä―•“Θ―•–Ζ / “™–Α―³―É –Β―² βÜ£ –Β―à―²–Β“Θ–Β –Β–Φ–Β―¹, –Φ–Β–Ι–Μ―•, ―²“·–Κ –Β―²–Ω–Β–Ι–¥―•
- `AskHowAreYou` βÄî “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι―¹―΄“Θ / “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι―¹―΄–Ζ / –Ε–Α“™–¥–Α–Ι―΄“Θ―΄–Ζ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι βÜ£ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ ―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―², –Ε–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ –Β–Φ–Β―¹–Ω―•–Ϋ, –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Α–Μ ―¹―•–Ζ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι―¹―΄–Ζ
- `StatementOfWellbeing` βÄî –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Φ―΄–Ϋ / –Ε–Α–Φ–Α–Ϋ –Β–Φ–Β―¹ βÜ£ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ, “¦―É–Α–Ϋ–Α–Φ―΄–Ϋ, –Α–Μ ―¹―•–Ζ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι―¹―΄–Ζ
- `AskName` βÄî –Α―²―΄“Θ –Κ―•–Φ / –Β―¹―•–Φ―•“Θ―•–Ζ “¦–Α–Μ–Α–Ι βÜ£ –Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –Α―²―΄–Φ –Α–¥–Α–Φ, –Φ–Β–Ϋ―• –Α–¥–Α–Φ –¥–Β–Ω –Α―²–Α–Ι–¥―΄

Templates are now loaded from `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` (14 families, one per intent-key), not hardcoded in `planner.rs`. `TemplateRepository::load_default()` auto-discovers the TOML file; `hardcoded_fallback()` preserves MVP guarantees when the file is missing.

Public API additions:

- `adam_dialog::TemplateRepository` + `TemplateError`
- `adam_dialog::respond_with_repo(input, lex, repo, seed)` βÄî explicit-repo variant of `respond`
- `adam_dialog::plan_response_with_repo(intent, seed, repo)` + `intent_key(intent)`

`adam_chat` REPL now loads the TOML repo at startup (falls back to hardcoded if missing) and prints family count on stderr.

Ordering subtlety in the semantic dispatcher: `Thanks`/`Apology` are checked BEFORE `Affirmation` so "―Ä–Α―Ö–Φ–Β―²" (thanks) can't accidentally fall into affirmation if later extended.

Tests: 23 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 15), 8 new covering all 5 new intents. Workspace totals: **183 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.7.5 limitations (by design, not bugs):

- Templates are still literal phrases; slotted templates with `(root, features)` atoms land in v0.8.0.
- No entity extraction (own name from "–Φ–Β–Ϋ―•“Θ –Α―²―΄–Φ X" βÜ£ greeting back by name).
- 10 intents cover greetings + basic social politeness; v0.8.0 widens to 25.
- No multi-turn state.

## [0.7.0] βÄî 2026-04-19

First iteration of the predictable dialog layer. New crate `adam-dialog` implements a 5-layer pipeline (FST parser βÜ£ semantics βÜ£ planner βÜ£ realiser βÜ£ FST synthesiser) against the architectural spec in `docs/kazakh_grammar/07_dialog_architecture.md`.

Recognises 5 intents from raw Kazakh input:
- `Greeting` with kind `Casual` / `Polite` / `TimeOfDay(Morning|Day|Evening)`
- `Farewell`
- `Affirmation`
- `Negation`
- `Unknown` (fallback)

Each intent has 2βÄ™4 hand-written response variants; planner picks one by seeded PRNG mod count. The entire output space is enumerable per input βÄî no free generation.

New binary `adam_chat` with three modes:
- `--once "<input>"` βÄî single-shot stdout response
- default βÄî interactive REPL over stdin
- `--trace` βÄî dump each layer's state (parses, intent, trace lines, output)

Tests: 15 end-to-end pairs cover the full pipeline. Workspace totals: 175 passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.7.0 limitations (by design, not bugs):
- Only 5 social intents; ~150 templates needed for v1.0.0 MVP.
- Templates are hardcoded in `planner.rs`, not data-driven TOML (v0.7.5).
- No morphological info used for intent classification yet (v0.7.5+).
- No multi-turn state.

## [0.6.0] βÄî 2026-04-19

Derivational morphology βÄî the "word-formation layer" the user flagged as a v1.0.0-path requirement. The FST now transforms a root into a new root via a derivational suffix before applying inflection. Eleven derivation types covered:

- `Agent` `-―à―΄/-―à―•` (–Ε–Α–Ζ―É βÜ£ –Ε–Α–Ζ―É―à―΄)
- `Abstract` `-–Μ―΄“¦/-–Μ―•–Κ` (–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ βÜ£ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄–Μ―΄“¦)
- `Privative` `-―¹―΄–Ζ/-―¹―•–Ζ` (―²“±–Ζ βÜ£ ―²“±–Ζ―¹―΄–Ζ)
- `Endowed` `-–Μ―΄/-–Μ―•` (–Κ“·―à βÜ£ –Κ“·―à―²―•)
- `Similative` `-–¥–Α–Ι/-–¥–Β–Ι` (―²–Α―É βÜ£ ―²–Α―É–¥–Α–Ι)
- `Comparative` `-―Ä–Α“¦/-―Ä–Β–Κ` (–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ βÜ£ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄―Ä–Α“¦)
- `VerbalNoun` `-―É` (–Ε–Α–Ζ βÜ£ –Ε–Α–Ζ―É)
- `ActionNoun` `-―΄–Φ/-―•–Φ` (–Α–Ι―² βÜ£ –Α–Ι―²―΄–Φ)
- `Diminutive` `-―à―΄“¦/-―à―•–Κ` (“·–Ι βÜ£ “·–Ι―à―•–Κ)
- `Ordinal` `-–Ϋ―à―΄/-–Ϋ―à―•` (–±―•―Ä βÜ£ –±―•―Ä―•–Ϋ―à―•)
- `Collective` `-–Β―É/-–Α―É` (–±―•―Ä βÜ£ –±―•―Ä–Β―É)

`NounFeatures` gains a `derivation: Option<Derivation>` field; `synthesise_noun` applies the derivation BEFORE inflection so the two pipelines chain correctly (–Ε–Α–Ζ―É βÜ£ Agent βÜ£ –Ε–Α–Ζ―É―à―΄ βÜ£ Dative βÜ£ –Ε–Α–Ζ―É―à―΄“™–Α).

Tests added: 10. `adam-kernel-fst` lib now at **78 passing**. Workspace at **160 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

No other code changes.

## [0.5.5] βÄî 2026-04-19

Pure Kazakh lexicon milestone. Enforces the "no loanwords" directive at the lexicon level and augments coverage from classical 19th-century sources.

Pipeline:

1. **Purity audit** (`lexicon_purity_audit` binary) βÄî classified all 16,373 entries from v0.4.0 curated + v0.4.5 Apertium-imported against strict pre-modern-Kazakh criteria (Russian-only letters, loanword suffixes, no Kazakh-specific letter).
2. **Pure Kazakh build** (`build_pure_kazakh_lexicon`) βÄî filtered out 1,500 contaminated entries (824 Russian letters, 128 loanword suffixes, 681 no-Kazakh-signal). Retained 13,606.
3. **Abai gap analysis** (`extract_abai_gap`) βÄî identified 715 unique root candidates missing from the lexicon but present as word forms in Abai's corpus.
4. **Augmentation** (`augment_lexicon_from_abai`) βÄî automatically classified the top 500 gap candidates (393 nouns + 107 verbs) with POS, vowel harmony, and final sound class. Output: `data/lexicon_v1/abai_augmented_roots.json`.

Result:

| metric | v0.5.0 | v0.5.5 |
|---|---|---|
| Lexicon entries (pure) | n/a | 14,106 |
| Loanwords dropped | 0 | 1,500 |
| Abai vocabulary coverage | 88.8% | **97.8%** (+9 pp) |

Missing-vocabulary examples added (each backed by corpus frequency):
- `―¹”©–Ζ` (word, speech βÄî 123Ο½ in Abai)
- `–±–Ψ–Ι`, `“¦–Α–Ϋ`, `“¦–Ψ–Μ`, `“¦–Α―Ä`, `–Ε–Α―É`, `–Ε–Α―²`, `–Ε–Β―²`, `―²“·―¹`, `“¦―΄―¹`, `–Ε–Α–Ι`
- `–Ϋ–Α–¥–Α–Ϋ` (ignorant βÄî Abai's key philosophical concept)

These are fundamental proto-Kazakh vocabulary items the Apertium import had zero entries for.

No changes to the FST code, phonology, or morphotactics modules. The augmented lexicon file lives alongside the v0.4.5 imports and can be unioned into the active lexicon at load time.

Workspace totals: 150 tests passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

## [0.5.0] βÄî 2026-04-19

Expands the v0.4.5 FST to cover Kazakh non-finite verb forms.

- **Vowel-final-stem aorist coalescence** (Apertium rules 17, 18, 19, 20, 30). Stem-final `―΄/―•` merge with the aorist `{A}` to produce `–Η` (e.g. `–Ψ“¦―΄` + PRES + 3 βÜ£ `–Ψ“¦–Η–¥―΄`, not the previous `*–Ψ“¦―΄–Α`). Stems ending in other vowels take a `–Ι`-glide (`―¹”©–Ι–Μ–Β` βÜ£ `―¹”©–Ι–Μ–Β–Ι–¥―•`). Past tense on vowel-final stems (`–Ψ“¦―΄` + PAST + 1SG βÜ£ `–Ψ“¦―΄–¥―΄–Φ`) continues to work without coalescence.
- **Participles** βÄî three new `Tense` variants:
  - `ParticiplePast` βÄî `-{G}{A}–Ϋ` (`–Ε–Α–Ζ“™–Α–Ϋ`, `–±–Β―Ä–≥–Β–Ϋ`, `“¦–Α–Μ“™–Α–Ϋ`).
  - `ParticipleHabitual` βÄî `-{A}―²―΄–Ϋ` (`–Ε–Α–Ζ–Α―²―΄–Ϋ`, `–Κ–Β–Μ–Β―²―•–Ϋ`).
  - `ParticipleFuture` βÄî `-{A}―Ä` (`–Ε–Α–Ζ–Α―Ä`, `–Κ–Β–Μ–Β―Ä`).
- **Converbs** βÄî two new `Tense` variants:
  - `ConverbPerfect` βÄî `-{Y}–Ω` (`–Ε–Α–Ζ―΄–Ω`, `–±–Β―Ä―•–Ω`).
  - `ConverbImperfect` βÄî `-{A}` (`–Ε–Α–Ζ–Α` without personal ending).

Tests: **68 unit tests passing** in `adam-kernel-fst` (up from 55 in v0.4.5). Workspace totals: 150 passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

No changes to v0.4.0 transformer baseline or v0.4.5 FST core code.

## [0.4.5] βÄî 2026-04-19

Introduces **adam-kernel-fst**, a pure-Rust deterministic finite-state transducer for Kazakh morphology. This is Phase 1 of the architecture pivot from stochastic transformers to deterministic morphology + small LM (v1.0.0 track). v0.4.0 transformer stack stays untouched; v0.4.5 adds the new FST layer alongside.

Highlights:

- **New crate `adam-kernel-fst`** βÄî phonology module (12 archiphonemes, 20+ of 54 Apertium twol rules implemented), morphotactics module (25 suffix templates covering noun plural/possessive/case and verb tense/voice/negation/person), parser module (`analyse(surface) βÜ£ Vec<(root, features)>`), lexicon loader (union of 4,454 curated + 11,919 Apertium-imported entries).
- **55 unit tests + 1 smoke test + 4 ignored slow roundtrips**. Slow tests (manual: `cargo test --test roundtrip -- --ignored`) roundtrip the full 14k lexicon on 4 feature combinations: **36,238 / 36,238 = 100.0 %** success.
- **CLI binary `adam_fst`** βÄî `synth`, `analyse`, `stats` subcommands. Hand-rolled arg parsing (no CLI-framework dep).
- **Apertium-kaz import pipeline** (`import_apertium_lexicon` binary) with POS mapping, loanword filter, and prefix-conflict guard.
- **Grammar study notes**: `docs/kazakh_grammar/00_architecture_v1.md`, `01_phonology.md`, `02_morphology.md`, `03_syntax.md`, `04_lexicon_sources.md`, `05_work_plan.md`, `06_apertium_twol_catalogue.md`.

Not yet:

- Vowel-final-stem edge cases (rule 17 coalescence, semivowel ―É).
- Participles, converbs, infinitive.
- LM over root + feature-bundle sequences (v0.5+ target).
- Replacement of v0.4.0 pipeline (deliberately left untouched).

Workspace totals: 137 tests passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing. CI green.

## [0.4.0] βÄî 2026-04-17

Corpus and infrastructure maturity release. Adds the first classical-literature source (Abai Qunanbayuly via Wikisource, 146 works, 2,253 samples), the first web-crawl source (CC-100 Kazakh, 50,000 samples filtered for Cyrillic-ratio and repetition), and fixes a data-composition bug in the synthetic generator (1- and 2-word outputs dominated the corpus, teaching the model early EOS). BPE retrained at vocab **8,192** with **3.27Ο½ compression** on a 12.5M-token pretokenized corpus. Model rolled back from the v0.4.0-failed experiment (27.3M, H=512 L=6) to **24.2M params** (H=512 L=5) after confirming that the L=6 scale-up was undertrained at 3.9M tokens.

Training: 20,000 steps, batch 8, seq 128, 3e-4 peak lr with cosine decay, 8h on M2 Metal at 0.64βÄ™0.70 steps/s throughput. First-class reliability: `train_baseline` now writes a periodic checkpoint every 2000 steps after a reboot lost 13k uncheckpointed steps mid-run.

Validation (honest):
- 12,101 held-out samples (larger/harder distribution vs v0.3.0's 1,939)
- mean_ce: 7.43, **perplexity: 1691.89**
- bits/char: **3.28** (v0.4.0-failed: 3.26; v0.3.0: 3.49 βÄî val sets not directly comparable)

Qualitative:
- Complete grammatical Kazakh sentences now appear in `temp=0.8` and nucleus samples (6 of 30 showcase outputs): `–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Α–¥–Α–Φ –Φ–Α“™―΄–Ϋ–Α –±–Β―Ä–Β–¥―•`, `–Ψ–Μ –Ε–Α–Ζ–Α–¥―΄`, `–Ψ–Μ–Α―Ä –Ε“·―Ä–Β–¥―•`, `“·–Μ–Κ–Β–Ϋ –Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Α–¥–Α–Φ –Ψ“¦―΄–Ι–¥―΄`, `–Φ–Β–Κ―²–Β–Ω ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄ –Φ”ô―Ä―²–Β–±–Β –Ϋ–Α“¦―²―΄–Μ–Α–Ι–¥―΄`.
- Greedy still terminates early βÄî expected for a capacity-bound model (24M params Ο½ 4M training tokens is ~25Ο½ below Chinchilla-optimal data).

v0.5.0 will address the data bottleneck: curriculum-style FSM expansion (L1/L2/L3 difficulty), larger CC-100 sample (50k βÜ£ 500k), classical-literature expansion (–ê―É―ç–Ζ–Ψ–≤, –ù―É―Ä–Ω–Β–Η―¹–Ψ–≤, –ë”©–Κ–Β–Ι locally), and SFT on translated Alpaca for the first instruction-following pass.

## [0.3.0] βÄî 2026-04-15

First capacity scale-up. `ModelConfig::tiny` grows from 4.28M βÜ£ **20.0M params** (hidden 224βÜ£512, layers 4βÜ£5, ffn 896βÜ£2048, head_dim 28βÜ£64). 15,000 training steps on the 39k unified corpus, 3h 45m on MacBook Air M2 Metal. Validation perplexity drops from **1112.31 βÜ£ 871.30 (βà£21.7%)** βÄî first meaningful delta since Phase 6a real-text onset. The 4.28M envelope was saturated at Phase 6d; Wikipedia + Common Voice distribution breadth required more model capacity. Peak RSS ~2.5 GB of 8 GB unified memory βÄî headroom confirmed.

## [0.2.0] βÄî 2026-04-15

First minor release after v0.1.0. Full retrain on the 39k unified corpus assembled across Phases 6aβÄ™6d. BPE retrained (3,336 merges, 2.80Ο½ compression, **0 unknowns, 100.00% roundtrip**). 4.28M model, 15,000 steps, 1h 48m wall time on M2 Metal. Val PPL 1078.68 βÜ£ 1112.31 (flat; val set is larger and harder βÄî capacity bottleneck now visible).

## [0.1.6] βÄî 2026-04-15

**Phase 6d βÄî Common Voice KK.** Mozilla Common Voice Kazakh sentence-collector integrated (6,108 accepted, CC0-1.0 text only). Unified corpus grows 32,986 βÜ£ 39,058 unique (+6,072; 4,282 duplicates dedup'd at assembly). Also fixes `scripts/bump_foundation_version.sh`: Cargo.lock is no longer perl-replaced (corrupted transitive deps under naive substring replace); regenerated by `cargo build` after Cargo.toml bump.

## [0.1.5] βÄî 2026-04-15

**Phase 7.1 βÄî Wikipedia-augmented retrain.** 4.28M baseline retrained on the corpus enlarged with Wikipedia KZ. 14,000 steps, ~2h on M2 Metal. Val PPL 626.81 βÜ£ 1078.68, reflecting a broader, harder val set (Wikipedia sentences are lexically richer than Tatoeba) βÄî honest baseline on the enlarged distribution, not a regression.

## [0.1.4] βÄî 2026-04-14

**Phase 6c βÄî Kazakh Wikipedia.** Plain-text extracted from the kk.wikipedia.org XML dump (~296k articles βÜ£ 15,000 clean samples after filter; CC-BY-SA 4.0). Unified corpus 17,986 βÜ£ 32,986 unique. Infrastructure: `scripts/fetch_wikipedia_kz.sh` (bzcat + perl streaming with UTF-8 fix), `process_wikipedia_kz` binary.

## [0.1.3] βÄî 2026-04-14

**Phase 7 βÄî first real-text baseline.** 4.28M model trained on the unified corpus including authentic Kazakh (Tatoeba): 7,000 steps, 61m on M2 Metal, dropout 0.05, grad clipping max-norm 1.0. Explicit `loss.backward() βÜ£ clip βÜ£ opt.step` replaces `opt.backward_step`. First honest perplexity on real text: **626.81** (vs 129.49 on pure synthetic βÄî tells us real Kazakh is harder).

## [0.1.2] βÄî 2026-04-14

BPE vocab size bumped 1390 βÜ£ **4096**. Char-level fallback + Tatoeba real text saturate the larger target.

## [0.1.1] βÄî 2026-04-14

**Phase 6a/6b βÄî first authentic Kazakh source.** Tatoeba Kazakh integrated (4,058 sentences, CC-BY 2.0 FR) via `fetch_tatoeba_kazakh.sh` + `process_tatoeba_kazakh`. Tokenizer adds char-level fallback for FSM-unknown words and leading-punct β•¹ marker placement βÄî brings roundtrip to 100% on mixed real/synthetic text.

## [0.1.0] βÄî 2026-04-14

First minor release. The foundation works end-to-end: a Kazakh-first 3.06M-parameter transformer language model trained from scratch on a self-generated, FSM-validated synthetic corpus, evaluated against held-out perplexity, and serving inference with morpheme-aware BPE encode/decode.

### Brand
- Logo `assets/shanraq.svg` integrated into README header.
- README rewritten with centered title, badges, quickstart, and stats.
- `AUTHORS` file added.

### Quality (cumulative since v0.0.85)
- Validation perplexity: **129.49** on a 699-sample held-out set (mean cross-entropy 4.86 over 2532 tokens).
- All 464 segmentation eval examples match at 10000 bps.
- Foundation validation green across 11 layers.

---

## Phase 5 βÄî Training and inference (v0.0.81 βÜ£ v0.0.92)

### [0.0.92] βÄî Phase 5i: Generation showcase report
- New `generation_showcase` binary: 20 prompts Ο½ 3 sampling configs = 60 generations.
- Report artifact `data/training/generation_showcase_report.json`.
- Foundation validation now requires showcase + perplexity reports.

### [0.0.91] βÄî Phase 5h: Top-p + repetition penalty
- `generate` gains nucleus (top-p) sampling and GPT-2-style repetition penalty.
- Backwards-compatible CLI; defaults are no-ops.

### [0.0.90] βÄî Phase 5g: Hyperparameter tuning
- Dropout 0.10 βÜ£ 0.05 reduces over-regularization on small corpus.
- Gradient clipping (max-norm 1.0) added to `train_baseline`.
- Training extended to 7000 steps with 300-step warmup.
- **Perplexity: 165.98 βÜ£ 129.49 (βà£22%).**

### [0.0.89] βÄî Phase 5f: Model scaling + dropout
- ModelConfig::tiny() bumped: hidden 192 βÜ£ 224, heads 6 βÜ£ 8, ffn 768 βÜ£ 896, +dropout=0.1.
- 2.33M βÜ£ 3.06M params.
- `forward(ids, train: bool)` added to gate dropout on/off.

### [0.0.88] βÄî Phase 5e: Held-out eval + perplexity
- `encode_corpus` extended with deterministic train/val split (FNV hash of sample id).
- New `eval_perplexity` binary writes structured `validation_perplexity_report.json`.
- First baseline: **165.98 perplexity**.

### [0.0.85] βÄî Phase 5d: Inference binary
- `generate` binary: load checkpoint, autoregressive sampling (greedy/temperature/top-k).
- First sentence generated by the model: "–Ε–Α“¦―¹―΄ –Α–¥–Α–Φ –Α–Ζ –Κ”©―Ä―¹–Β―²–Β–¥―•."

### [0.0.84] βÄî Phase 5c: Training loop
- `train_baseline` binary: AdamW + linear-warmup + cosine-decay LR + safetensors checkpointing.
- First trained checkpoint, training loss 7.94 βÜ£ 3.39 in 7m on Metal.

### [0.0.83] βÄî Phase 5b: Data loader
- `DataLoader` reads ids pack, produces shifted (input, target) batches on device.
- End-to-end smoke test: forward + cross-entropy loss.

### [0.0.81] βÄî Phase 5a: Candle integration
- Added candle (HuggingFace Rust ML) with Metal backend on macOS, CPU elsewhere.
- `AdamBaseline` decoder-only transformer (initial 2.21M params).
- M2 Metal smoke test passes.

---

## Phase 4 βÄî Tokenizer (v0.0.78 βÜ£ v0.0.80)

### [0.0.87] βÄî Phase 4d+4e: Lexicon-seeded BPE
- BPE vocab now seeded with all 211 lexicon roots + all 422 rule forms before counting pairs.
- 0% `<unk>` on any FSM-parseable Kazakh word.

### [0.0.80] βÄî Phase 4c: BPE encoder/decoder
- `bpe::BpeTokenizer` module: load vocab+merges, encode text βÜ£ ids, decode ids βÜ£ text.
- `encode_corpus` binary writes a training-ready ids pack.
- 100% round-trip on 7,737 samples.

### [0.0.79] βÄî Phase 4b: BPE trainer
- `train_bpe` binary: iterative most-frequent-pair merging over morpheme stream.
- Skips merges across word boundary (right token starts with β•¹).
- 567 merges learned from corpus statistics; 2.12Ο½ compression.

### [0.0.78] βÄî Phase 4a: Pre-tokenizer
- `pretokenize(text, lexicon, rules)`: morpheme-aware splitting via FSM.
- SentencePiece-style β•¹ marker on word-start morphemes.
- Handles standalone punctuation and whole-word fallback.

---

## Phase 3 βÄî Corpus (v0.0.74 βÜ£ v0.0.77)

### [0.0.86] βÄî Phase 3e: Full POS coverage
- 15 βÜ£ 30 templates exercising every POS (adverbs, particles, modals, –Ψ–Μ/–Ψ–Μ–Α―Ä, conjunctions).
- Synthetic corpus 10,000 βÜ£ 18,000 samples.
- Unified corpus 7,737 βÜ£ 13,929 unique samples.

### [0.0.77] βÄî Phase 3d: Kazakh proverbs
- Added 80 classical –Φ–Α“¦–Α–Μ-–Φ”ô―²–Β–Μ–¥–Β―Ä across 23 themes.
- Proverbs bypass FSM-validation policy (archaic morphology); Cyrillic-only check.

### [0.0.76] βÄî Phase 3c: Unified corpus
- `assemble_unified_corpus` binary: dedup + renumber across packs.
- 7,657 unique samples from 10,094 inputs.

### [0.0.75] βÄî Phase 3b: Rich templates
- Generator templates 6 βÜ£ 15: pronouns with matched person, conjunctions, multi-argument, etc.
- 10,000 sentences (95% yield).

### [0.0.74] βÄî Phase 3a: Synthetic generator
- `synth_sentences` binary: combines FSM lexicon and rules to produce grammatically valid Kazakh sentences.
- Self-validation: every generated word verified by `deterministic_segment_token`.
- FSM fix: removed vowel from `verb_tense_a/e_from_stem` allowed finals (linguistically correct βÄî `–Ι` handles vowel-final aorist).

---

## Phase 2 βÄî Grammatical foundation (v0.0.66 βÜ£ v0.0.73)

### [0.0.73] βÄî Phase 2h: Modals
- New `Modal` POS, 6 roots: –Κ–Β―Ä–Β–Κ, –Φ“·–Φ–Κ―•–Ϋ, ―²–Η―•―¹, ―à―΄“™–Α―Ä, “¦–Α–Ε–Β―², –Μ–Α–Ι―΄“¦.

### [0.0.72] βÄî Phase 2g: Nominal predicate
- 16 predicative personal suffix rules: -–Φ―΄–Ϋ/–Φ―•–Ϋ, -―¹―΄“Θ/―¹―•“Θ, -―¹―΄–Ζ/―¹―•–Ζ, -–Φ―΄–Ζ/–Φ―•–Ζ on noun + adjective.
- 3 copula bare lexemes as Particle: –Β–¥―•, –Β–Κ–Β–Ϋ, –Β–Φ–Β―¹.

### [0.0.71] βÄî Phase 2f: Adverbs
- New `Adverb` POS, 19 roots: “¦–Α–Ζ―•―Ä, –±“·–≥―•–Ϋ, –Β―Ä―²–Β“Θ, –Κ–Β―à–Β, ―²–Β–Ζ, –±–Α―è―É, –Ε–Ψ“¦, –Η”ô, etc.

### [0.0.70] βÄî Phase 2e: Numerals
- New `Numeral` POS, 20 cardinals: –±―•―ÄβÄ™–Ε“·–Ζ, –Φ―΄“Θ.
- 4 ordinal suffix rules: -–Ϋ―à―΄/–Ϋ―à―•/-―΄–Ϋ―à―΄/―•–Ϋ―à―•.

### [0.0.69] βÄî Phase 2d: Conjunctions + Particles
- New `Conjunction` POS, 9 roots: –Ε”ô–Ϋ–Β, –±―•―Ä–Α“¦, ―¹–Β–±–Β–±―•, ”©–Ι―²–Κ–Β–Ϋ―•, etc.
- New `Particle` POS, 12 roots: –Φ–Α/–Φ–Β, –±–Α/–±–Β, –Ω–Α/–Ω–Β, “™–Ψ–Ι, –¥–Α/–¥–Β, ―²–Β–Κ, “¦–Α–Ϋ–Α, ”©―²–Β.

### [0.0.68] βÄî Phase 2c: Roots + 3sg aorist
- 29 nouns, 13 verbs, 5 adjectives added.
- Critical FSM fix: `tense βÜ£ person_3sg` was missing for aorist forms (e.g. –±–Β―Ä–Β–¥―• = –±–Β―Ä+–Β+–¥―•). Added rules for both future and negative_future predecessors.
- "–Ι" connector rule for vowel-final verb stems (–Ε–Α―¹–Α–Ι–¥―΄).
- Coverage 19.79% βÜ£ **73.77%** on educational corpus.

### [0.0.67] βÄî Phase 2b: Postpositions
- New `Postposition` POS, 15 roots: –Α―Ä“¦―΄–Μ―΄, “·―à―•–Ϋ, ―²―É―Ä–Α–Μ―΄, –Κ–Β–Ι―•–Ϋ, etc.

### [0.0.66] βÄî Phase 2a: Adjectives
- New `Adjective` POS, 25 roots, 57 inflection rules (mirror of noun rules).
- Coverage 4.56% βÜ£ 17.93%.
